description: Single-directional circular pattern feature that creates copies of selected features around a specified axis. Supports equal or variable spacing between instances. The axis is pre-selected (mark 1) along with features to pattern (mark 4) before calling CreateDefinition. When EqualSpacing is true, the Spacing property defines the total angle; when false, it defines the angle between the first two instances.

description: Create a single-directional circular pattern with custom angular spacing based on pre-selected seed features. When EqualSpacing is false, the Spacing property defines the angle between consecutive instances rather than distributing instances equally across a total angle. This allows for basic non-uniform angular distribution. For per-instance spacing customization, InstancesToVary must be enabled (requires GeometryPattern=false) and configured via IInstanceToVaryOptions after feature creation.

description: Creating a single-directional circular pattern feature that distributes pattern instances at equal angular spacing around a selected axis, where the pattern is based on faces (not features or bodies). The axis must be pre-selected, and faces are assigned programmatically. When EqualSpacing is enabled, the Spacing parameter defines the total angular span (in radians) across all instances, not the spacing between individual instances.

description: Creates a bidirectional circular pattern feature where pattern instances are distributed equally in both directions around a specified axis. When EqualSpacing/EqualSpacing2 are true, the Spacing/Spacing2 parameters define the total angular span over which instances are evenly distributed, not the spacing between each instance. Direction2 must be true and Symmetric must be false to independently control spacing in Direction 2.

description: A bidirectional circular pattern feature where Direction 2 creates symmetric instances around the pattern axis. When Symmetric=true, Direction 2 instances are mirrored around the seed, and only TotalInstances2 can be configured (Spacing2 and EqualSpacing2 are not available). This feature replicates selected entities (features, faces, or bodies) in a circular arrangement with symmetrical distribution.

description: Create a circular pattern feature and subsequently modify it to vary the spacing and dimensions of specific instances. This feature requires a two-phase approach: first create the circular pattern normally, then modify the existing feature to enable instance variation with custom spacing and dimension modifications for individual pattern instances in one or both directions. Instance variation only works when GeometryPattern is false.

description: A simple through-all hole created using HoleWizard5 with standard-based sizing. The hole extends completely through the part without counterboring, countersinking, or threading. For standard holes, the diameter is determined by the SSize parameter from the hole wizard database, while the Diameter parameter is ignored (set to 0). The through-all end condition means the hole penetrates the entire part thickness, making the Depth parameter irrelevant. Screw fit (Value1) controls clearance when using standard sizes and defaults to NORMAL_FIT if not specified. All other Value parameters are set to -1 as they apply to other hole types.

specialized_signature:
```csharp
// Simple through-all hole using standard size database
// The diameter is determined by SSize parameter, not Diameter parameter
// Input angles in RADIANS, dimensions in METERS

feature = featureMgr.HoleWizard5(
    (int)swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdHole,        // GenericHoleType: 2 = simple/regular hole
    (int)swWzdHoleStandards_e.<standard>,          // StandardIndex: must match FastenerTypeIndex
                                                    //   e.g., swStandardAnsiInch, swStandardAnsiMetric, swStandardISO
    (int)swWzdHoleStandardFastenerTypes_e.<type>,  // FastenerTypeIndex: MUST match standard or error occurs
                                                    //   e.g., swStandardAnsiInchAllDrillSizes, swStandardAnsiMetricFlatHead82
    "<size>",                                      // SSize: MUST be valid size for fastener type or error occurs
                                                    //   e.g., "M2", "#4", "#97" - determines actual hole diameter
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondThroughAll,    // EndType: 1 = through-all (hole penetrates entire part)
    0.0,                                            // Diameter: IGNORED when using standard sizes (SSize determines diameter)
                                                    //   Only used if SSize is empty/custom hole
    0.0,                                            // Depth: IGNORED for through-all end condition
    0.0,                                            // Length: NOT applicable for simple holes (slots only), set to 0
    
    // Value1-Value12 for simple holes (set unused to -1):
    1,                                              // Value1: Screw Fit (swWzdHoleScrewClearanceTypes_e)
                                                    //   0 = swScrewClearanceClose
                                                    //   1 = swScrewClearanceNormal (NORMAL_FIT - default)
                                                    //   2 = swScrewClearanceLoose
                                                    //   Only used if Diameter not specified; -1 uses default NORMAL_FIT
    -1,                                             // Value2: Drill Angle at bottom (radians)
                                                    //   NOT VALID for swEndCondThroughAll or swEndCondUpToNext
                                                    //   MUST be -1 for through-all holes
                                                    //   Defaults to 118° (2.05948851735331 rad) if -1 for blind holes
    -1,                                             // Value3: Near Csink Diameter (meters) - not used for simple holes
    -1,                                             // Value4: Near Csink Angle (radians) - not used for simple holes
    -1,                                             // Value5: Far Csink Diameter (meters) - not used for simple holes
    -1,                                             // Value6: Far Csink Angle (radians) - not used for simple holes
    -1,                                             // Value7: Offset (meters) - only valid for swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
                                                    //   MUST be -1 for through-all holes
    -1,                                             // Value8: ALWAYS ignored, set to -1
    -1,                                             // Value9: ALWAYS ignored, set to -1
    -1,                                             // Value10: ALWAYS ignored, set to -1
    -1,                                             // Value11: ALWAYS ignored, set to -1
    -1,                                             // Value12: ALWAYS ignored, set to -1
    
    "",                                             // ThreadClass: Empty string (not applicable for simple holes)
                                                    //   Only used for ANSI inch tapped holes: "1B", "2B", "3B"
    false,                                          // RevDir: false for normal direction (see docs for reference plane logic)
    true,                                           // FeatureScope: true to affect only selected bodies in multibody part
    true,                                           // AutoSelect: true to automatically select all bodies
    true,                                           // AssemblyFeatureScope: true for assembly feature (affects selected components)
    true,                                           // AutoSelectComponents: true to auto-select all affected components
    false                                           // PropagateFeatureToParts: false (don't propagate to individual parts)
);

// CRITICAL VALIDATION:
// - FastenerTypeIndex MUST match StandardIndex or error occurs
// - SSize MUST be valid for the FastenerTypeIndex or error occurs
// - Value2 and Value7 MUST be -1 for through-all end condition
// - For reference planes with material on both sides: RevDir logic is complex (see HoleWizard4 docs)
```

description: Creates a simple cylindrical blind hole that terminates at a specified depth without penetrating through the part. This basic hole type (swWzdHole general type, classified as swHoleBlind=22 after creation) is commonly used for pilot holes, clearance holes, and fastener applications. The hole has a conical drill point at the bottom defined by the drill angle parameter.

specialized_signature:
// IMPORTANT: Must pre-select face or sketch point for hole location before calling HoleWizard5
// Use SelectByRay (Mark=0) or SelectByID2 for location selection

// Simple blind hole - GenericHoleType: swWzdHole creates simple holes
// After creation, Type property returns swHoleBlind = 22
Feature swFeat = swFeatMgr.HoleWizard5(
    (int)swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdHole,  // = 2 for simple holes
    (int)swWzdHoleStandards_e.swStandardAnsiMetric,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandardFastenerTypes_e.swStandardAnsiMetricAllDrillSizes,
    "M2",
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,  // = 0
    0.002,  // Diameter in meters
    0.010,  // Depth in meters
    -1,  // Length: NOT used for holes
    
    // Regular Holes parameter pattern:
    1,  // Value1: Screw Fit (swWzdHoleScrewClearanceTypes_e: 0=Close, 1=Normal, 2=Loose)
    2.0595,  // Value2: Drill Angle (radians, default 118deg = 2.0595 rad)
    -1,  // Value3: Near Csink Diameter
    -1,  // Value4: Near Csink Angle
    -1,  // Value5: Far Csink Diameter
    -1,  // Value6: Far Csink Angle
    -1,  // Value7: Offset
    -1, -1, -1, -1, -1,  // Value8-12: unused
    
    "",  // ThreadClass
    false,  // RevDir
    true, true, false, false, false
);

// Valid properties: HoleDiameter, HoleDepth, DrillAngle, Length (slot only)

description: A tapered tap hole created using IFeatureManager::HoleWizard5 with swWzdPipeTap (value 3). Tapered tap holes are threaded pipe holes following standards like NPT (National Pipe Thread Tapered), BSPT (British Standard Pipe Tapered), and others. These features create threads at a taper angle following pipe thread standards. The hole includes a tap drill diameter, thread depth, thread angle (typically 1.79 degrees for NPT), and optional cosmetic thread representation.

description: Create counterbored holes and counterbored slots using the HoleWizard5 method. Counterbored holes combine a primary hole with a counterbore section that enlarges the hole to accommodate screw heads or fasteners. Modern counterbore holes support additional countersink options for near, middle, and far side features. The method accepts parameters defining the hole's geometry, counterbore dimensions, optional countersink features, drill angle, and end conditions. The actual hole type created determines which IWizardHoleFeatureData2 properties are valid for accessing hole parameters.

[HoleWizard4.md] - Obsolete but documents parameter structure and RevDir parameter logic for reference planes
specialized_signature:
feature_mgr.HoleWizard5(
    GenericHoleType: (int)swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdCounterBore,
    StandardIndex: (int)swWzdHoleStandards_e./* valid standard */,
    FastenerTypeIndex: (int)swWzdHoleStandardFastenerTypes_e./* must match standard */,
    SSize: "/* valid size string */",
    EndType: (short)swEndConditions_e./* swEndCondBlind=0, swEndCondThroughAll=1 */,
    Diameter: /* double - primary hole diameter in meters */,
    Depth: /* double - primary hole depth in meters */,
    Length: /* double - slot length (REQUIRED for swWzdCounterBoreSlot) */,
    Value1: /* double - CounterBore Diameter */,
    Value2: /* double - CounterBore Depth */,
    Value3: /* double - Head Clearance ADDED to counterbore depth */,
    Value4: /* double - Screw Fit (0=Close, 1=Normal, 2=Loose) */,
    Value5: /* double - Drill Angle in radians (IGNORED if EndType is ThroughAll or UpToNext) */,
    Value6: /* double - Near Csink Diameter or -1 */,
    Value7: /* double - Near Csink Angle or -1 */,
    Value8: /* double - Underhead Csink Diameter or -1 */,
    Value9: /* double - Underhead Csink Angle or -1 */,
    Value10: /* double - Far Csink Diameter or -1 */,
    Value11: /* double - Far Csink Angle or -1 */,
    Value12: /* double - Offset if EndType is OffsetFromSurface */,
    ThreadClass: "2B",
    RevDir: false,
    FeatureScope: false,
    AutoSelect: true,
    AssemblyFeatureScope: false,
    AutoSelectComponents: true,
    PropagateFeatureToParts: true
);

// Properties for swCounterBoreBlind: HoleDiameter, HoleDepth, CounterBoreDiameter, CounterBoreDepth, DrillAngle
// Properties for swCounterBoreThru: ThruHoleDiameter, ThruHoleDepth, CounterBoreDiameter, CounterBoreDepth
// With countersink variants add: NearCounterSinkDiameter/Angle, MidCounterSinkDiameter/Angle, FarCounterSinkDiameter/Angle
// Legacy swCounterBored: Diameter, Depth, CounterBoreDiameter, CounterBoreDepth

description: The counterdrilled hole is a legacy hole type that creates a hole with a cylindrical counterdrill depression cut at an angle at the bottom of the main hole. It is created using HoleWizard5 with swWzdLegacy as GenericHoleType. Two variants exist: swCounterDrilled (simple counterdrilled) and swCounterDrilledDrilled (counterdrilled with bottom drill angle). Properties include Diameter, Depth, CounterDrillDiameter, CounterDrillDepth, CounterDrillAngle, and optionally DrillAngle for the drilled variant.

description: Creates a blind straight/pipe tapped hole where the thread depth can be explicitly specified. This feature requires specifying the tap drill diameter, tap drill depth, and thread depth. If tap drill depth is not specified, SOLIDWORKS auto-calculates it from the thread length plus advance plus allowance for tap type (bottoming or plug).

[Table_of_Wizard_Hole_Types_and_Valid_Properties.md] - Defines valid IWizardHoleFeatureData2 properties for swTapBlind: TapDrillDiameter, TapDrillDepth, ThreadDiameter, ThreadDepth (note: DrillAngle is NOT listed)

[SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.IFeatureManager~HoleWizard2.md] - Obsolete documentation showing the correct parameter sequence for tap holes: 1. Tap drill diameter, 2. Tap drill depth, 3. Thread Depth, plus information on auto-calculation behavior

[SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.IFeatureManager~HoleWizard4.md] - Obsolete documentation explaining tap drill depth calculation: 'For tapped holes: Tap drill depth is calculated from the thread length + advance + allowance for tap type (bottoming or plug)' and thread depth calculation: 'For tapped holes: based on 2 * thread diameter'
example_code_snippets:
// Example 1: Create blind straight tap hole with explicit thread depth
// IMPORTANT: There is a parameter order discrepancy between HoleWizard5 and
// obsolete documentation. This example follows HoleWizard5 current docs.
// If hole creation fails, try the obsolete parameter order (see Example 3).

Feature holeFeature = featureManager.HoleWizard5(
    (int)swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdTap,          // Straight/pipe tap (NOT tapered)
    (int)swWzdHoleStandards_e.swStandardAnsiInch,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandardFastenerTypes_e.swStandardAnsiInchMachineScrew,
    "#10-24",
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    0.0,                // Diameter - ignored for tap holes
    0.020,              // Depth - 20mm total hole depth
    -1.0,               // Length - ignored for holes
    0.015,              // Value1 - Tap Thread Depth: 15mm threaded depth (per HoleWizard5 docs)
    -1.0,               // Value2 - Near Csink Diameter (not used)
    -1.0,               // Value3 - Near Csink Angle (not used)
    -1.0, -1.0, -1.0,   // Value4-6 unused for basic tap
    (int)swWzdHoleCosmeticThreadTypes_e.swCosmeticThreadWithCallout,  // Value7 - show cosmetic thread
    (int)swWzdHoleThreadEndCondition_e.swEndThreadTypeBLIND,          // Value8 - blind thread
    -1.0, -1.0, -1.0, -1.0,  // Value9-12 unused
    "2B",               // ThreadClass - "2B" for normal fit
    false, false, true, false, false, false
);

// Example 2: Create metric tap hole with auto-calculated tap drill depth
// When tap drill depth (Value2 per obsolete docs, or not specified) is not provided,
// SOLIDWORKS calculates it from: thread length + advance + allowance for tap type

Feature metricTapHole = featureManager.HoleWizard5(
    (int)swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdTap,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandards_e.swStandardAnsiMetric,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandardFastenerTypes_e.swStandardAnsiMetricSocketHeadCap,
    "M6",
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    0.0,
    0.025,              // 25mm total depth
    -1.0,
    0.018,              // Value1 - 18mm thread depth (per current docs)
    -1.0,               // Value2 - tap drill depth auto-calculated by SOLIDWORKS
    -1.0, -1.0, -1.0, -1.0,
    0,                  // Value7 - no cosmetic thread
    0,                  // Value8 - blind thread end condition
    -1.0, -1.0, -1.0, -1.0,
    "",                 // ThreadClass - ignored for metric
    false, false, true, false, false, false
);

// Example 3: ALTERNATIVE parameter order based on obsolete documentation
// Use this if Example 1 doesn't work. Obsolete docs show:
// Value1=Tap drill diameter, Value2=Tap drill depth, Value3=Thread Depth

Feature alternativeOrder = featureManager.HoleWizard5(
    (int)swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdTap,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandards_e.swStandardAnsiInch,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandardFastenerTypes_e.swStandardAnsiInchMachineScrew,
    "#10-24",
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    0.0,
    0.020,
    -1.0,
    0.00508,            // Value1 - Tap drill diameter: 0.2" (per obsolete docs)
    0.018,              // Value2 - Tap drill depth: 18mm (per obsolete docs)
    0.015,              // Value3 - Thread Depth: 15mm (per obsolete docs)
    -1.0, -1.0, -1.0,   // Value4-6
    0,                  // Value7 - Cosmetic Thread Type
    0,                  // Value8 - Thread End Condition
    -1.0, -1.0, -1.0, -1.0,
    "2B",
    false, false, true, false, false, false
);

description: A tapped through hole with thread depth (swTapThru = 33) is created using IFeatureManager::HoleWizard5 with swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdTap. This creates a threaded hole that extends completely through the part (swEndCondThroughAll) with a user-specified thread depth controlling the length of the threaded portion. The Diameter parameter specifies the tap drill diameter, while Value1 (Thread Depth) controls how far the threading extends. When thread depth is specified by the user, it overrides the default calculated value (normally 2 times thread diameter for tapped holes). The ThruTapDrillDepth property is geometry-dependent for through holes and should not be set programmatically; instead, it is determined by the part thickness.

specialized_signature:
// Tapped through hole with thread depth (swTapThru = 33)
Feature feature = featureMgr.HoleWizard5(
    (int)swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdTap,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandards_e.swStandardISO,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandardFastenerTypes_e.swStandardISOMetricMachineScrew,
    "M6",
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondThroughAll,
    0.0050,  // Tap drill diameter (meters)
    -1.0,    // Depth (ignored for through-all)
    -1.0,    // Length (must be -1 for tap holes)
    0.010,   // Value1: Thread depth (meters), overrides default
    -1.0, -1.0, -1.0, -1.0,  // Value2-5: Countersink params (ignored)
    2.059488517353,  // Value6: Drill angle (radians)
    (int)swWzdHoleCosmeticThreadTypes_e.swCosmeticThreadNone,  // Value7
    (int)swWzdHoleThreadEndCondition_e.swEndThreadTypeBLIND,   // Value8
    -1.0, -1.0, -1.0, -1.0,  // Value9-12
    "2B", false, false, true, false, false, false
);

// Valid properties for swTapThru:
// ThruTapDrillDiameter, ThruTapDrillDepth (read-only), ThreadDiameter, ThreadDepth

description: Creates a pipe-tapped hole with tapered threads (NPT/NPTF standards) using the Hole Wizard feature. Pipe-tapped holes use swWzdPipeTap (value 3) which specifically refers to tapered pipe threads commonly used in plumbing and pneumatic connections where the taper creates a seal. This is distinct from straight tap holes (swWzdTap, value 4). The feature supports blind and through-all end conditions with optional countersink variations.

description: A tapped hole feature with cosmetic thread representation creates a blind tapped hole that includes a visual cosmetic thread without actual thread geometry. The cosmetic thread type (none, with callout, or without callout) controls the visual representation. SOLIDWORKS calculates tap drill depth and thread depth from standard data unless explicitly specified by the user, with different calculation methods for helicoil vs. regular tapped holes.

specialized_signature:
// Create a blind tapped hole with cosmetic thread representation
// CRITICAL: For tap holes, macro recorder fails to record Length parameter correctly.
// You must manually add -1 for Length parameter and remove one -1 before ThreadClass.

IFeatureManager featureMgr = swModel.FeatureManager;

Feature feature = featureMgr.HoleWizard5(
    GenericHoleType: (int)swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdTap,
    StandardIndex: (int)swWzdHoleStandards_e.swStandardISO,
    FastenerTypeIndex: (int)swWzdHoleStandardFastenerTypes_e.swStandardISOMetricTap,
    SSize: "M6",
    EndType: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    Diameter: 0.005,  // Tap drill diameter in meters (NOT thread diameter)
    Depth: 0.020,     // Total hole depth in meters
    Length: -1,       // MUST be -1 for tap holes (macro recorder bug)
    Value1: 0.015,    // Tap Thread Depth (threaded portion). If -1: calculated
    Value2: -1,       // Near Csink Diameter (unused)
    Value3: -1,       // Near Csink Angle (unused)
    Value4: -1,       // Far Csink Diameter (unused)
    Value5: -1,       // Far Csink Angle (unused)
    Value6: 2.0595,   // Drill angle (radians, ~118 degrees)
    Value7: (int)swWzdHoleCosmeticThreadTypes_e.swCosmeticThreadWithCallout,
    Value8: (int)swWzdHoleThreadEndCondition_e.swEndThreadTypeBLIND,
    Value9: -1,       // Helicoil Tap Type (helicoil only)
    Value10: -1,      // Offset (unused)
    Value11: -1,      // Unused
    Value12: -1,      // Unused
    ThreadClass: "", // Only for ANSI inch standard
    RevDir: false,
    FeatureScope: false,
    AutoSelect: false,
    AssemblyFeatureScope: false,
    AutoSelectComponents: false,
    PropagateFeatureToParts: false
);

// Access feature definition
IWizardHoleFeatureData2 holeData = (IWizardHoleFeatureData2)feature.GetDefinition();
holeData.CosmeticThreadType;  // swWzdHoleCosmeticThreadTypes_e
holeData.TapDrillDiameter;    // Tap drill diameter (meters)
holeData.TapDrillDepth;       // Tap drill depth (meters)
holeData.ThreadDiameter;      // Thread diameter (meters)
holeData.ThreadDepth;         // Thread depth (meters)
holeData.ThreadEndCondition;  // swWzdHoleThreadEndCondition_e
holeData.DrillAngle;          // Drill angle (radians)
holeData.Type;                // Returns swTapBlindCosmeticThread (46)

description: A countersink hole without head clearance is created using GenericHoleType = swWzdCounterSink (1) from swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e enum with HeadClearance parameter (Value3) set to 0. After creation, IWizardHoleFeatureData2::Type property returns swCounterSinkBlindWithoutHeadClearance (43) or swCounterSinkThruWithoutHeadClearance (44) from swWzdHoleTypes_e enum.

// Use swWzdCounterSink as GenericHoleType, NOT WithoutHeadClearance values!
feature = featureManager.HoleWizard5(
    (int)swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdCounterSink,  // GenericHoleType: always use swWzdCounterSink (1)
    (int)swWzdHoleStandards_e.swStandardAnsiMetric,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandardFastenerTypes_e.swStandardAnsiMetricFlatHead82,
    "M2",
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,  // or swEndCondThroughAll
    0.0102,  // Diameter in meters
    0.008,   // Depth in meters
    0.0,     // Length (holes only, not slots)
    0.0044,  // Value1: NearCounterSinkDiameter
    1.5708,  // Value2: NearCounterSinkAngle in radians
    0.0,     // Value3: HeadClearance = 0 for without head clearance
    -1,      // Value4: ScrewFit
    -1,      // Value5: DrillAngle
    -1,      // Value6: FarCsinkDiameter
    -1,      // Value7: FarCsinkAngle
    -1,      // Value8: Offset
    -1,      // Value9: HeadClearanceType (ignored when Value3=0)
    -1, -1, -1,  // Value10-12: unused
    "", false, true, true, true, true, false
);
// After creation: holeData.Type returns 43 (swCounterSinkBlindWithoutHeadClearance)

description: Creating a Hole Wizard hole with the 'Up To Surface' end condition allows the hole to terminate precisely at a selected surface, face, plane, edge, or vertex. The surface must be pre-selected using SelectByID2 with Mark=2 before calling HoleWizard5 with swEndCondUpToSelection (value 10). When using a reference plane as the terminating surface, the RevDir parameter must be properly determined based on material placement: if material is on one side of the plane, RevDir is false; if on both sides, compare distances and set RevDir to true if the distance along the plane's normal direction is greater. The macro recorder may incorrectly record this end condition as value 4 or 3 instead of 10.

description: Creates a Hole Wizard hole or slot that extends to a selected vertex using IFeatureManager::HoleWizard5 with swEndCondUpToSelection (10) end condition. The vertex must be pre-selected using SelectByID2 with Mark=2 before calling HoleWizard5. To later access or modify the vertex reference, call AccessSelections to roll back the feature, then use GetEndConditionReference or SetEndConditionReference methods on the feature data object.

description: A simple straight hole slot created using the Hole Wizard API (IFeatureManager::HoleWizard5). This feature creates a straight rectangular slot through or into a part at specified locations. The slot is defined by its diameter (width of circular ends), depth (for blind slots), length (rectangular extension), and end condition. The Diameter parameter sets the hole width at each end, while Length defines the slot's rectangular extension between the ends. For simple straight slots without counterbore or countersink features, use GenericHoleType swWzdHoleSlot (8) with appropriate Value1-Value7 parameters. Note: Screw fit (Value1) should be -1 when diameter is explicitly specified; the Length parameter requires manual correction in macro recordings due to a known macro recorder bug.
source_documents: [SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.IFeatureManager~HoleWizard5.md] - Primary method documentation for HoleWizard5, including parameter descriptions, remarks section documenting that screw fit is 'only used if a hole or slot diameter is not specified' (meaning -1 when diameter IS specified), macro recorder bugs for Length parameter requiring manual correction, default drill angle of 118 degrees (2.05948851735331 radians), and Value1-Value12 parameter mappings for Regular Holes and Straight Slots

description: A counterbored slot is a slot feature with an enlarged/counterbored section that can accommodate screw heads. Created using HoleWizard5 method (introduced in SOLIDWORKS 2014) with swWzdCounterBoreSlot generic hole type. The Length parameter is mandatory for all slot types and specifies the slot's linear extent. Counterbore slots support various countersink options (top, middle, bottom) through Value6-Value11 parameters.

description: A countersunk slot is created using the HoleWizard5 method with GenericHoleType = swWzdCounterSinkSlot (7). It creates a slot with a countersunk cone-shaped cut at one or both ends. The resulting feature's IWizardHoleFeatureData2.Type property returns values like swCounterSinkSlotBlind (71), swCounterSinkSlotThru (76), etc., depending on the end condition and countersink configuration. The method directly creates the feature with control over slot geometry including length, diameter, depth, countersink parameters (near/far diameters and angles), head clearance, and end conditions.

description: Creating a Hole Wizard hole with offset from surface end condition (swEndCondOffsetFromSurface = 5), which positions the hole at a specified distance from a reference surface, plane, or face. The offset distance parameter position varies by hole type: Value12 for counterbore and pipe/straight tap holes, Value8 for countersink and tapered tap holes, and Value7 for regular holes. The reference surface must be set via IWizardHoleFeatureData2::SetEndConditionReference after feature creation.

description: A counterbored hole with a near-side (top surface) countersink created using the Hole Wizard. This variant combines a main counterbored hole with a countersink specifically on the near (top) surface only. Unlike variants with mid/underhead or far-side countersinks, this configuration only supports the near-side countersink parameters, making it suitable for applications requiring flush fastener seating on the entry side of a component.

description: This feature instance creates a through-hole with a counterbore on the near (top) side and a countersink on the far (bottom/exit) side. The hole consists of two main elements: (1) a through-hole section with an enlarged counterbore portion for fastener head clearance on the entry side, and (2) a conical countersink depression on the exit side. This configuration is commonly used when fasteners require clearance for the head on one side and need to sit flush or below the surface on the opposite side.

description: A blind tapped hole with a near-side countersink created using the Hole Wizard (swTapBlindCounterSinkTop = 32). This hole type includes a tap drill hole with threads extending to a specified depth, and a countersink machined on the near (top) surface. The hole uses tap-specific drilling parameters (tap drill diameter and depth) separate from standard hole dimensions, with thread depth controlling the extent of threading within the tap drill hole.

example_code_snippets:
// WARNING: These examples reflect current HoleWizard5 docs but may need adjustment
// based on obsolete documentation parameter mapping discrepancies

// Example 1: M6 blind tapped hole with near-side countersink (metric ANSI)
// Using current HoleWizard5 parameter interpretation
swFeat = swFeatMgr.HoleWizard5(
    (int)swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdTap,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandards_e.swStandardAnsiMetric,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandardFastenerTypes_e.swStandardAnsiMetricMachineScrew,
    "M6",
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    0.005,      // Diameter param - may be tap drill diameter
    0.025,      // Depth param - may be tap drill depth  
    -1,         // Length (not used for tap holes)
    0.010,      // Value1: Tap thread depth
    0.008,      // Value2: Near countersink diameter
    0.785398,   // Value3: Near countersink angle (45°)
    -1, -1,     // Value4-5: Far countersink (not used)
    0.01745329, // Value6: Drill angle (default)
    0,          // Value7: Cosmetic thread type (none)
    0,          // Value8: Thread end condition (blind)
    -1, -1, -1, -1,  // Value9-12
    "", false, false, true, false, true, false);

// Example 2: 1/4-20 UNC tapped hole with near countersink (ANSI inch)
swFeat = swFeatMgr.HoleWizard5(
    (int)swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdTap,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandards_e.swStandardAnsiInch,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandardFastenerTypes_e.swStandardAnsiInchMachineScrew,
    "1/4-20",
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    0.0045,     // #7 drill size for 1/4-20 UNC
    0.030,      // Tap drill depth
    -1,
    0.015,      // Value1: Thread depth
    0.0625,     // Value2: Near countersink diameter  
    0.785398,   // Value3: 45° angle
    -1, -1, -1,
    0,          // Value7: No cosmetic thread
    0,          // Value8: Blind thread end
    -1, -1, -1, -1,
    "2B",       // ThreadClass for inch standard
    false, false, true, false, true, false);

// Example 3: Using DIN standard with proper face selection
boolstatus = swModelDocExt.SelectByID2("Face1", "FACE", 0, 0, 0, false, 0, null, 0);
swFeat = swFeatMgr.HoleWizard5(
    (int)swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdTap,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandards_e.swStandardDIN,  
    (int)swWzdHoleStandardFastenerTypes_e.swStandardDinMachineScrew,
    "M10",
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    0.0085,     // Tap drill diameter
    0.040,      // Tap drill depth
    -1,
    0.020,      // Thread depth
    0.015,      // Near countersink diameter
    0.785398,   // 45° countersink angle
    -1, -1, -1, 0, 0, -1, -1, -1,
    "", false, true, true, false, true, false);

// NOTE: Actual parameter values may need adjustment based on verification
// of the parameter mapping inconsistency between current and obsolete docs

description: A tapped hole with thread-through end condition creates threads that extend completely through the part thickness. This feature uses the HoleWizard5 method with GenericHoleType set to swWzdTap (4), and critically requires ThreadEndCondition (Value10) to be set to swEndThreadTypeTHROUGH_ALL (1) or swEndThreadTypeTHROUGH_NEXT (2) instead of the default swEndThreadTypeBLIND (0). The hole wizard creates both the tap drill hole and threads, with the threads extending based on the specified end condition rather than a fixed depth.

specialized_signature:
// Thread-Through Tapped Hole using IFeatureManager.HoleWizard5
// CRITICAL: GenericHoleType MUST be swWzdTap (4), NOT swWzdCounterSink
// CRITICAL: Value10 (ThreadEndCondition) MUST be 1 or 2 for thread-through

Feature holeFeature = featureManager.HoleWizard5(
    (int)swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdTap,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandards_e.swStandardISO,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandardFastenerTypes_e.swStandardISOMetricMachine,
    "M6",
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondThroughAll,
    0.006,       // Diameter
    0.030,       // Depth: controls ThruHoleDepth
    -1.0,        // Length: see macro recorder note in remarks
    0.0053,      // Value1: TapDrillDiameter (ThruTapDrillDiameter)
    0.025,       // Value2: TapDrillDepth (ThruTapDrillDepth)
    0.020,       // Value3: ThreadDepth
    0.0,         // Value4: Near Csink Diameter
    0.0,         // Value5: Near Csink Angle
    0.0,         // Value6: Far Csink Diameter
    0.0,         // Value7: Far Csink Angle
    0.0,         // Value8: DrillAngle
    (int)swWzdHoleCosmeticThreadTypes_e.swCosmeticThreadNone,  // Value9
    (int)swWzdHoleThreadEndCondition_e.swEndThreadTypeTHROUGH_ALL,  // Value10: CRITICAL
    -1.0,        // Value11: Helicoil Tap Type
    0.0,         // Value12: Offset
    "",          // ThreadClass
    false, true, true, false, false, false
);

description: Modifying existing Hole Wizard hole properties is performed by retrieving the IWizardHoleFeatureData2 object from an existing IFeature using GetDefinition or IGetDefinition, modifying its properties, and then applying the changes using ModifyDefinition or IModifyDefinition2. For simple property modifications (diameters, depths, angles), only GetDefinition/ModifyDefinition are required. However, to access geometric references (Face, IFace, Vertex, IVertex) or use GetEndConditionReference, you must first call AccessSelections to put the model into a rollback state, then call ReleaseSelectionAccess if not modifying, or ModifyDefinition which automatically restores the rollback state.

description: Creates a Split feature that splits a part into multiple bodies using cutting geometry (planes, surfaces, sketches) and optionally saves selected bodies to separate part documents. The feature supports both solid body and surface splitting (added in SOLIDWORKS 2015). Bodies can be consumed (removed from the original part) or retained, and each body can have a custom origin point and save path.

description: Creates a Split feature that keeps selected bodies visible in the original part without saving them to separate documents. The bodies remain in the part's Solid Bodies folder and are not consumed. This is achieved by setting consumeCut=false (to keep bodies in the part) and using empty savePaths strings (to not save to separate files).

description: Modifying an existing Split feature by retrieving its ISplitBodyFeatureData definition object, altering properties (Consume flag, template settings), and applying changes via ModifyDefinition. For modifications involving the split bodies themselves or trim tools, AccessSelections must be called first to enter rollback mode, then either ModifyDefinition or ReleaseSelectionAccess must be called to restore state. After ModifyDefinition, check for rebuild errors using GetErrorCode2.

sldworksapi/SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.ISplitBodyFeatureData~Consume.md - Boolean property controlling whether split bodies are consumed (removed from original part); available since SOLIDWORKS 2007 FCS

sldworksapi/SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.ISplitBodyFeatureData~OverrideDefaultTemplateSettings.md - Boolean property to enable custom template usage; available since SOLIDWORKS 2018 FCS

sldworksapi/SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.ISplitBodyFeatureData~AccessSelections.md - Gains access to Split feature selections by entering rollback mode; MUST be followed by either ModifyDefinition (if modified) or ReleaseSelectionAccess (if not) to restore state

sldworksapi/SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.ISplitBodyFeatureData~ReleaseSelectionAccess.md - Releases rollback state without applying changes; use when AccessSelections was called but no modifications were made

sldworksapi/SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.ISplitBodyFeatureData~GetSplitBodies.md - Returns three arrays (BodyVar: IBody2 objects, PathVar: save paths, FlagVar: consume flags) representing current split body configuration; call before SetSplitBodies2 to inspect/modify current state

sldworksapi/SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.ISplitBodyFeatureData~SetSplitBodies2.md - Modifies split bodies using arrays of paths, consume flags, and body origins; must be called AFTER GetSplitBodies to change split body configuration; available since SOLIDWORKS 2015 FCS

sldworksapi/SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.ISplitBodyFeatureData~TrimTools.md - Get-only property returning array of trimming surfaces (faces, planes, surfaces, sketches); cannot be modified via property setter in .NET

sldworksapi/SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.ISplitBodyFeatureData~ISetTrimTools.md - Sets trim tools but NOT SUPPORTED in VBA, VB.NET, C#, or C++/CLI - only available for in-process unmanaged C++

sldworksapi/SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.IFeature~GetDefinition.md - Retrieves feature data object for inspection/modification; returns System.Object that must be cast to ISplitBodyFeatureData; set to null when no longer needed

sldworksapi/SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.IFeature~ModifyDefinition.md - Applies modified feature data to feature; may leave feature in unpredictable state on failure - check GetErrorCode2 afterward; leaves assembly in Editing Part mode when modifying component features

description: This feature instance describes how to modify an existing Split feature by retrieving its ISplitBodyFeatureData definition, updating properties such as consumption behavior and template settings, modifying the split bodies configuration via SetSplitBodies2, and then applying those changes back to the feature. For modifications involving split body selections, AccessSelections must be called first to place the model in rollback state. Critical: Always check for errors using GetErrorCode2 after ModifyDefinition, as the method may return true while still causing rebuild errors.

description: A solid revolve feature that rotates a closed sketch profile about a selected axis in a single direction with a blind end condition (specified by an angle in radians). This creates a boss/base feature by sweeping the profile 360° or less around the axis. The sketch must form a closed contour for solid revolves.

description: This feature case creates a solid boss/base revolve feature using a mid-plane end condition (swEndCondMidPlane), which revolves a sketch profile around an axis with the revolution distributed equally on both sides of the sketch plane. When Dir1Type is set to swEndCondMidPlane (value 6), the revolve creates a symmetrical feature about the sketch plane. Note that swEndCondMidPlane (the end condition parameter) is distinct from swRevolveTypeMidPlane (the Type property value 1 in IRevolveFeatureData2). The angle behavior with mid-plane end conditions requires clarification as different SOLIDWORKS API methods document different angle handling.

Supports blind angles, mid-plane, up-to selection (use swEndCondUpToSelection=10, NOT deprecated swEndCondUpToSurface=4), offset from surface, up-to next, up-to body, and through-all. description: Creates a solid boss or base revolve feature with an offset from surface end condition by calling FeatureRevolve2(). This feature revolves a sketch profile around an axis, with one or both directions terminating at a specified offset distance from a reference surface, face, plane, or edge. When using two directions with the same offset entity, the entity must be selected once with BothDirectionUpToSameEntity=true to avoid negating the selection. This end condition was introduced in FeatureRevolve2 to supersede older revolve methods that lacked advanced end condition support.

description: A thin revolve feature with mid-plane wall type creates a revolved solid or cut feature where the wall thickness is applied symmetrically (divided equally on both sides of the sketch profile). The key parameter is ThinWallType=swThinWallMidPlane (2), which causes ThinThickness1 to be automatically divided by 2 and applied as thickness/2 on each side of the sketch. This thin wall configuration is independent of the Dir1Type end condition and can be combined with blind, mid-plane, up-to surface, or other end conditions.

description: A thin revolve feature created in two directions using FeatureRevolve2, where wall thickness can be independently specified for each direction (ThinType = swThinWallTwoDirection = 3). This allows different thickness values (ThinThickness1 for Direction 1, ThinThickness2 for Direction 2) to be applied perpendicular to the sketch profile as it revolves around the axis. Note that SingleDir parameter controls revolve end conditions (angles/up-to surfaces) while ThinType controls wall thickness application direction - these are independent settings.

description: A cut revolve feature with single direction removes material from a solid body by revolving a selected sketch profile around an axis in one direction only. This is a subtractive operation that uses a single revolution angle for blind end conditions or extends up to a specified surface or vertex. When cut features result in multiple bodies, all resulting bodies are kept; individual body selection is not supported. For thin-wall variants, a constant thickness is applied to the profile during revolution.

specialized_signature:
// Cut revolve feature with single direction - IMPORTANT: For cut features resulting in multiple bodies, all bodies are kept; you cannot select to keep only specific resulting bodies.
IFeature feature = featureMgr.FeatureRevolve2(
    SingleDir: true,
    IsSolid: true,
    IsThin: false,  // Set true for thin-wall with constant thickness
    IsCut: true,
    ReverseDir: false,  // NOTE: Has no effect if Dir1Type=swEndCondMidPlane
    BothDirectionUpToSameEntity: false,
    Dir1Type: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,  // swEndCondBlind=0, swEndCondMidPlane=6, swEndCondUpToSelection=10, swEndCondOffsetFromSurface=5, swEndCondThroughAll=1
    Dir2Type: 0,
    Dir1Angle: 6.28318530718,  // Radians, only applies if Dir1Type=swEndCondBlind
    Dir2Angle: 0,
    OffsetReverse1: false,
    OffsetReverse2: false,
    OffsetDistance1: 0,  // Only if Dir1Type=swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
    OffsetDistance2: 0,
    ThinType: 0,  // Only if IsThin=true: swThinWallOneDirection=0, swThinWallMidPlane=2
    ThinThickness1: 0,
    ThinThickness2: 0,
    Merge: true,
    UseFeatScope: false,  // True to affect only selected bodies
    UseAutoSelect: true  // False requires Mark=1 body selections
);

// Pre-selections required: Sketch (Mark 0), Axis (Mark 4 or 16), Optional surface (Mark 32), Optional bodies (Mark 1)
// Ignored: Dir2Type, Dir2Angle, OffsetReverse2, OffsetDistance2, BothDirectionUpToSameEntity

description: A cut revolve feature that removes material in two opposite directions from the revolve axis, useful for creating symmetric cuts around a center plane. This feature supports multiple end conditions (blind angle, mid-plane, up-to selection, offset from surface) in each direction. Note: swEndCondUpToSurface and swEndCondUpToVertex are deprecated; use swEndCondUpToSelection instead. When using cut features that result in multiple bodies, you cannot selectively keep specific resulting bodies.

description: A thin cut revolve feature removes material from a part by rotating an open or closed sketch profile around an axis, with controlled wall thickness applied perpendicular to the sketch geometry. This operation combines cutting with thin-wall modeling, supporting single-direction, two-direction, and mid-plane configurations with various end conditions (blind, up-to-selection, offset-from-surface). The feature can affect all bodies or only selected bodies in multi-body parts.

description: This feature case creates a revolve feature in a multibody part that affects only specific selected bodies rather than all bodies. description: This feature instance represents an assembly revolve feature with component scope, created via FeatureRevolve2 with UseFeatScope equals true. In an assembly context, UseFeatScope enables component-level targeting where the revolve affects only selected or auto-selected components rather than all assembly parts. Assembly-specific behavior is controlled via IRevolveFeatureData2 properties: AssemblyFeatureScope, AutoSelectComponents, and PropagateFeatureToParts.

description: Modifying an existing revolve feature by accessing its definition, adjusting the revolution type and angle using IRevolveFeatureData2 interface, then applying changes back to the feature. This use case focuses on editing revolve feature parameters like Type (one-direction, two-direction, or mid-plane revolutions with optional 360-degree variants) and SetRevolutionAngle/GetRevolutionAngle (angle in either direction) after gaining access to the feature's selections. Critical workflow: must call either ModifyDefinition or ReleaseSelectionAccess after AccessSelections to restore rollback state.

description: This feature case demonstrates modifying an existing revolve feature by retrieving its IRevolveFeatureData2 definition, gaining access to its selections via AccessSelections(), modifying properties like Type, Axis, ReverseDirection, revolution angle (in radians), wall thickness, and contours, and finally applying changes via IFeature::ModifyDefinition(). For multibody parts, feature scope properties (FeatureScope, FeatureScopeBodies, AutoSelect, Merge) enable selective body targeting. CRITICAL: All revolution angles are specified in radians, not degrees.

specialized_signature: IFeature feature = ...; IRevolveFeatureData2 revolveData = (IRevolveFeatureData2)feature.GetDefinition(); bool success = revolveData.AccessSelections(topDoc, component); try { revolveData.Type = (int)swRevolveType_e.swRevolveTypeTwoDirection360Degrees; revolveData.Axis = axisObject; revolveData.ReverseDirection = true; revolveData.SetRevolutionAngle(true, Math.PI); revolveData.SetRevolutionAngle(false, Math.PI/4); revolveData.Contours = new object[] { contour1, contour2 }; revolveData.ThinWallType = (int)swThinWallType_e.swThinWallMidPlane; revolveData.SetWallThickness(true, 0.05); revolveData.SetWallThickness(false, 0.03); revolveData.FeatureScope = true; revolveData.AutoSelect = false; revolveData.FeatureScopeBodies = new object[] { body1, body2 }; revolveData.Merge = true; bool modified = feature.ModifyDefinition(revolveData, topDoc, component); } finally { }
example_code_snippets: ModelDoc2 swModel = swApp.ActiveDoc; Feature swFeat = swModel.FeatureManager.GetFeatureByName("Revolve1"); RevolveFeatureData2 revolveData = (RevolveFeatureData2)swFeat.GetDefinition(); revolveData.AccessSelections(swModel, null); revolveData.Type = (int)swRevolveType_e.swRevolveTypeTwoDirection; revolveData.SetRevolutionAngle(true, 2.0944); revolveData.SetRevolutionAngle(false, 1.0472); swFeat.ModifyDefinition(revolveData, swModel, null); revolveData.AccessSelections(swModel, null); revolveData.Axis = newAxisEdge; revolveData.ReverseDirection = true; swFeat.ModifyDefinition(revolveData, swModel, null);

description: Modifies an existing thin revolve feature by accessing its definition through IFeature::GetDefinition() and calling AccessSelections() to unlock the feature's selections for editing. This use case focuses on modifying wall thickness properties using SetWallThickness() and the ThinWallType property. The behavior of SetWallThickness depends on ThinWallType: for swThinWallMidPlane, only the forward thickness is used and divided by 2 for each direction; for swThinWallTwoDirection, both forward and reverse thicknesses are used; for swThinWallOneDirection and swThinWallOppDirection, only one thickness applies. After modifications, either call IFeature::ModifyDefinition() to apply changes or ReleaseSelectionAccess() to restore the rollback state without changes.

description: Creates a uniform thickness inward shell feature by removing pre-selected faces and applying a constant thickness directed inward. The InsertFeatureShell method is called with Outward=false (which results in Direction=0 in the resulting IShellFeatureData) to create the shell. After creation, the IShellFeatureData properties reflect the parameters used but are read-only unless the feature is being modified through the AccessSelections/ModifyDefinition workflow.

description: This feature case documents creating a uniform thickness shell feature with outward direction using the direct insertion method. The shell is created by removing selected faces from a solid body and applying a single, uniform thickness value that extends outward from the original solid surfaces. The Outward=true parameter ensures material is added to the outside of the remaining geometry (Direction=1), as opposed to inward shells where material is removed from the inside (Direction=0).

description: Creates a shell feature with different thicknesses applied to multiple selected faces using IModelDoc2::InsertFeatureShellAddThickness in conjunction with IModelDoc2::InsertFeatureShell. This workflow requires selecting faces to remove (Mark=1), then selecting multiple faces for alternate thicknesses (Mark=2), calling InsertFeatureShellAddThickness once for each Mark=2 face in selection order to assign individual thickness values, and finally calling InsertFeatureShell with the default thickness and direction.

example_code_snippets:
```csharp
// Example 1: Creating a multi-thickness shell with 3 different thicknesses (C#)
ModelDoc2 part = swApp.ActiveDoc;
part.ClearSelection2(true);

// Select two faces to remove (Mark=1)
part.Extension.SelectByRay(-7.62012496738862E-03, 2.99999999999159E-02, 
    2.14688454421434E-02, -0.400036026779312, -0.515038074910024, 
    -0.758094294050284, 7.62535384051002E-04, 2, false, 1, 0);
part.Extension.SelectByRay(-1.46810566606064E-02, 2.48617446113713E-02, 
    4.60521566346301E-02, -0.400036026779312, -0.515038074910024, 
    -0.758094294050284, 7.62535384051002E-04, 2, true, 1, 0);

// Select 3 faces with alternate thicknesses (Mark=2)
// These will receive thicknesses of 50mm, 30mm, and 70mm respectively
part.Extension.SelectByRay(4.38932166734958E-02, 2.08576911617797E-02, 
    2.73030283917137E-02, -0.400036026779312, -0.515038074910024, 
    -0.758094294050284, 7.62535384051002E-04, 2, false, 2, 0);
part.Extension.SelectByRay(4.89432166734958E-02, 2.58576911617797E-02, 
    3.23030283917137E-02, -0.400036026779312, -0.515038074910024, 
    -0.758094294050284, 7.62535384051002E-04, 2, true, 2, 0);
part.Extension.SelectByRay(5.39432166734958E-02, 3.08576911617797E-02, 
    3.73030283917137E-02, -0.400036026779312, -0.515038074910024, 
    -0.758094294050284, 7.62535384051002E-04, 2, true, 2, 0);

// Set thickness for each alternate-thickness face in selection order
part.InsertFeatureShellAddThickness(0.05);  // 50mm for 1st face
part.InsertFeatureShellAddThickness(0.03);  // 30mm for 2nd face
part.InsertFeatureShellAddThickness(0.07);  // 70mm for 3rd face

// Create the multi-thickness shell with 10mm default thickness (inward)
part.InsertFeatureShell(0.01, false);
```

```vb
' Example 2: Accessing and modifying multi-thickness shell properties (VB.NET)
Dim swModel As ModelDoc2 = swApp.ActiveDoc
Dim swSelMgr As SelectionMgr = swModel.SelectionManager
Dim swFeat As Feature = swSelMgr.GetSelectedObject6(1, -1)
Dim swShell As ShellFeatureData = swFeat.GetDefinition()

' Access shell properties
Dim thickness As Double = swShell.Thickness  ' Default thickness
Dim direction As Integer = swShell.Direction  ' 0=inward, 1=outward
Dim altFaceCount As Integer = swShell.GetMultipleThicknessFacesCount()

Debug.Print("Shell has " & altFaceCount & " faces with alternate thickness")

' Access selection data to read/modify multi-thickness information
If swShell.AccessSelections(swModel, Nothing) Then
    Dim vMultiFaceArr As Object = swShell.MultipleThicknessFaces
    
    ' Read alternate thickness values (indices match selection order during creation)
    For i As Integer = 0 To altFaceCount - 1
        Dim altThickness As Double = swShell.GetMultipleThicknessAtIndex(i)
        Debug.Print("Face " & i & " alternate thickness: " & _
                    altThickness * 1000 & " mm")
    Next
    
    ' Modify alternate thickness at index 1 (second face selected with Mark=2)
    swShell.SetMultipleThicknessAtIndex(1, 0.04)  ' Change to 40mm
    
    ' Apply modification
    swFeat.ModifyDefinition(swShell, swModel, Nothing)
    swShell.ReleaseSelectionAccess()
End If
```

description: Modify an existing shell feature by retrieving its IShellFeatureData object via IFeature::GetDefinition, adjusting properties (thickness, direction, removed faces, or multi-thickness faces), then applying changes through IFeature::ModifyDefinition. Must call AccessSelections before modifying face arrays to enter rollback state. Always verify success with GetErrorCode2 as ModifyDefinition may return true but leave feature with rebuild errors.

description: Creates a constant radius fillet feature with a symmetric profile that can be circular, conic rho, or conic radius. This fillet type maintains a uniform default radius along selected edges but can optionally apply different radii to different edges when IsMultipleRadius is set to true. The conic rho values are automatically clamped to the valid range [0.05, 0.95] if out-of-range values are specified. Created by initializing ISimpleFilletFeatureData2 with swConstRadiusFillet, configuring the cross-sectional profile type, selecting edges (or faces/features/loops), and setting radius parameters.

description: This feature creates a symmetric constant radius fillet with different radius values applied to individual selected edges. This requires selecting at least two adjacent edges with Mark=1, initializing with swConstRadiusFillet, setting IsMultipleRadius to true, and using SetRadius to assign custom radii to each edge. The cross-sectional profile MUST be swFeatureFilletCircular (conic profiles are not supported with multiple radii). CurvatureContinuous must be false when using multiple radii.

description: Creates a symmetric face fillet between two sets of faces with a specified profile (circular, conic rho, or conic radius). The fillet blends the two face sets using the selected cross-sectional profile. When CurvatureContinuous is true, it creates a smoother curvature that overrides the ConicTypeForCrossSectionProfile setting.

description: This feature instance creates an asymmetric face fillet between two distinct face sets with custom profile control. The fillet blends between Face Set 1 (Mark=2) and Face Set 2 (Mark=4) with different radii on each side (Distance 1 and Distance 2). Valid cross-section profiles for asymmetric face fillets are circular (elliptical) or conic rho only; conic radius is not supported for asymmetric fillets. Conic rho values outside [0.05, 0.95] are automatically corrected to the nearest valid value.

description: A face fillet with chord width profile creates a smooth transition between two faces where the fillet cross-section is defined by a chord width measurement (the straight-line distance across the fillet profile) rather than a radius. This differs from symmetric/asymmetric face fillets where the radius defines the curvature. The chord width profile can be circular, conic rho, or conic radius, and is particularly useful when you need to control the width of the fillet blend rather than its radius. The fillet is created using ISimpleFilletFeatureData2 initialized with swFaceFillet type, with ConicTypeForCrossSectionProfile set to the desired profile type and DefaultRadius specifying the chord width value.

description: Creates a face fillet with tangent hold line boundary constraints between two face sets. The hold lines define edges that constrain the fillet boundary, with the fillet shape determined by the specified edge. When using tangent hold lines, the radius of the fillet is driven by the distance between the hold line and the edge to be filleted, providing precise control over fillet placement and shape.

description: A face fillet feature that creates a smooth blend between two sets of faces using a curvature continuous profile. Unlike standard face fillets with circular or conic profiles, curvature continuous fillets ensure no jump in curvature at the boundaries between the fillet surface and adjacent faces, resulting in superior surface quality. This profile type is particularly useful for Class-A surfacing and high-quality aesthetic surfaces.

description: A full round fillet creates a rounded blend between three face sets: two side face sets and one center face set. The fillet is created by initializing ISimpleFilletFeatureData2 with swFullRoundFillet type, selecting the three face sets (either via SelectByID2 with marks 2, 512, and 4, or via SetFaces() with enum values 3, 4, 5), setting the profile type to swFeatureFilletCircular, and optionally configuring propagation to tangent faces before creating the feature.

description: An offset face chamfer with symmetric profile is a chamfer feature applied to selected edges with either a uniform offset distance or multiple offset distances. The symmetric profile indicates that the chamfer has uniform characteristics perpendicular to the edge (as opposed to asymmetric chamfers which have different offsets on each side). This feature is created using ISimpleFilletFeatureData2 initialized with swConstRadiusFillet type and configured with swFeatureFilletConicRhoZeroChamfer profile type.

description: Creates a face-face chamfer feature with asymmetric profile between two face sets using swFaceFillet initialization type and swFeatureFilletConicRhoZeroChamfer profile type. This allows creating chamfers between two faces with different offset distances on each side, enabling asymmetric chamfer geometry. Unlike offset face chamfers (which are edge-based and use swConstRadiusFillet), face-face chamfers require selecting two distinct face sets.

description: Creates a face-face chamfer between two distinct face sets using the swFeatureFilletConicRhoZeroChamfer profile type. This feature requires initializing with swFaceFillet type, assigning two face sets using SetFaces method with swFaceFilletSet1 and swFaceFilletSet2 enum values, and specifying offset distance(s). Supports both symmetric (single offset distance) and asymmetric (two different offset distances) chamfer configurations.

description: A setback fillet feature that allows variable setback distances at corner vertices of a constant radius fillet. Uses a per-vertex, per-edge approach where specific distances are set for each edge emanating from selected setback vertices, allowing precise control over where the fillet is reduced or removed at corner points. Requires selecting edges to fillet, then specifying setback vertices and their associated edge distances.

description: A partial edge fillet applies fillet segments to portions of specified edges rather than the entire edge. Edges must first be selected using Mark=1, then EnablePartialEdgeParameters is set to true, and IPartialEdgeFilletData is obtained for each selected edge to configure start/end conditions (distance offsets, percentage offsets, or reference-based offsets from sketch points, reference points, or planar faces).

description: This feature case applies a constant radius fillet to at least two adjacent selected edges with corner rounding enabled. Corner rounding creates smooth transitions at vertices where multiple filleted edges meet. This feature requires CurvatureContinuous to be false and uses a circular cross-sectional profile. The selected edges must share common vertices for the corner rounding to take effect.

description: Creates a mirror pattern feature by mirroring selected features, faces, solid bodies, or structure systems about a specified plane or planar face. Selection requires pre-marking entities (features=1, faces=128, bodies=256, structure systems=134217728, mirror plane/face=2). The BMirrorBody parameter determines whether to mirror features/faces (false) or solid bodies (true). After creation via InsertMirrorFeature2, the feature definition accessed through IMirrorPatternFeatureData allows querying the mirror plane type, mirrored geometry, patterned features, and scope settings with geometry pattern constraints.

description: Mirror pattern with geometry pattern mode mirrors only the feature geometry (faces and edges) rather than solving the entire feature about a selected plane or planar face. This mode is useful for duplicating geometric shapes without recalculating feature dependencies, which can improve performance. The feature is created using InsertMirrorFeature2 with BGeometryPattern set to true. Important: The FeatureScope and FeatureScopeBodies properties are only valid when GeometryPattern is true.

description: Creates a mirror pattern feature using IFeatureManager::InsertMirrorFeature2. The BKnit parameter (True/False) controls knitting of mirrored surfaces at creation time, but this setting cannot be queried or modified afterward via IMirrorPatternFeatureData (unlike IMirrorSolidFeatureData which has a KnitSurface property). This method requires pre-selected and marked entities (features mark=1, faces mark=128, bodies mark=256, structure systems mark=134217728, planes/planar faces mark=2) and directly creates the mirror feature without explicitly instantiating a feature data object. The feature data is subsequently accessed via Feature::GetDefinition() which returns IMirrorPatternFeatureData.

description: Creates a solid lofted protrusion (boss) feature by blending between multiple profiles selected in order. For this profiles-only case, no guide curves or centerline are used, producing a smooth transition between the cross-sectional profiles. The profiles must be closed contours (not open sketches) because a solid body is being created. Selection order of profiles determines the loft direction.

description: Creates a lofted protrusion (boss) feature by blending between multiple profile sketches with optional guide curves and centerline. Guide curves control the loft surface between profiles, and a centerline can specify the loft path. Multiple guide curves require a specific compound selection mark pattern (4098, 8194, 12290, etc.) rather than simple mark=2. Returns an IFeature whose ILoftFeatureData definition can be accessed via GetDefinition().

Insert_Protrusion_Blend_Example_VBNET.md - Shows compound marks (4098, 8194, 12290, 16386) for four guide curves, mark=4 for centerline. This contradicts the stated mark=2 for guide curves in the method documentation.

specialized_signature:
// Feature creation via InsertProtrusionBlend2
// CRITICAL: Multiple guide curves require compound selection marks
// Pattern: mark = 2 + (4096 * zero_based_index)
// First guide: 4098, Second: 8194, Third: 12290, Fourth: 16386

// Selection process before InsertProtrusionBlend2:
// Profiles: mark=1, Guide curves: mark=4098/8194/12290/etc, Centerline: mark=4
// Start tangent vector: mark=8, End tangent vector: mark=32

IFeature loftFeature = featureManager.InsertProtrusionBlend2(
    closed,                // bool - true closes loft (requires 3+ profiles OR closed guides)
    keepTangency,         // bool - maintains tangency of section curves
    forceNonRational,     // bool - true for smoother non-rational surfaces
    tessToleranceFactor,  // double - default 1.0, controls intermediate sections
    startMatchingType,    // short - 0=none, 1=normal, 2=vector, 3=all_faces (4=not available)
    endMatchingType,      // short - 0=none, 1=normal, 2=vector, 3=all_faces (4=not available)
    startTangentLength,   // double - start tangent magnitude
    endTangentLength,     // double - end tangent magnitude
    startTangentDir,      // bool - start tangent direction
    endTangentDir,        // bool - end tangent direction
    isThinBody,           // bool - true for thin-wall loft
    thickness1,           // double - first direction thickness (if isThinBody)
    thickness2,           // double - second direction thickness (if isThinBody and thinType=3)
    thinType,             // short - swThinWallType_e: 0=one_dir, 1=opp_dir, 2=midplane, 3=two_dir
    merge,                // bool - merge result in multibody parts
    useFeatScope,         // bool - feature affects only selected bodies
    useAutoSelect,        // bool - auto-select all bodies
    guideCurveInfluence   // int - swGuideCurveInfluence_e: 0=NextGuide, 1=NextSharp, 2=NextEdge, 3=NextGlobal
);

// Access ILoftFeatureData
ILoftFeatureData loftData = loftFeature.GetDefinition() as ILoftFeatureData;
loftData.Close;                   // bool
loftData.Profiles;                // object - IFeature array
loftData.GuideCurves;            // object - IFeature array
loftData.Centerline;             // object - ICenterLine
loftData.StartTangencyType;      // short - swTangencyType_e
loftData.EndTangencyType;        // short - swTangencyType_e
loftData.GuideCurveInfluence;    // int - swGuideCurveInfluence_e
loftData.ThinWallType;           // short - swThinWallType_e
loftData.IsBossFeature();        // method - returns true
loftData.GetProfileCount();      // method
loftData.GetGuideCurvesCount();  // method

description: Creates a solid lofted protrusion (boss) feature by calling IFeatureManager.InsertProtrusionBlend2() with preselected profiles, optional guide curves (strongly recommended), and optional centerline. The method blends multiple cross-sectional profiles into a smooth 3D shape. Selections must be made via SelectByID2 with specific marks (1=profiles, 2=guide curves, 4=centerline) before calling this method. After creation, the feature definition can be accessed via IFeature.GetDefinition() which returns ILoftFeatureData for querying or modifying loft properties.

Specifies selection mark requirements: 1=profiles, 2=guide curves, 4=centerline, 8=start tangency vector, 32=end tangency vector. Notes guide curves are strongly recommended.

description: Creates a closed lofted protrusion (boss) feature from multiple cross-sectional profiles. When Closed=true, the loft automatically connects the last profile back to the first profile, creating a sealed solid body. IMPORTANT: Profiles must be closed curves for solid lofts. If fewer than 3 profiles are used with Closed=true, any guide curves must also be closed. Commonly used for creating enclosed organic forms like bottles or vases.

description: Creates a lofted protrusion (boss) feature with tangency constraints at the start and/or end profile specified at creation time. Tangency type is controlled by StartMatchingType/EndMatchingType parameters (0=none, 1=normal to profile, 2=direction vector, 3=all adjacent faces) passed to InsertProtrusionBlend2(). For direction vector tangency (type 2), the vector geometry must be pre-selected with marks 8 (start) or 32 (end). After creation, the tangency type is read-only but tangent length and direction can be modified via ILoftFeatureData properties.

description: Creates a lofted protrusion (boss) feature with thin wall geometry applied in one direction. This feature type lofts through multiple closed cross-sectional profiles (closed curves are required for solid features) with optional guide curves and centerline, and applies a uniform wall thickness in the forward feature direction, creating a thin-walled solid structure. The Closed parameter controls whether the loft path itself closes (end profile connects to start profile), which is independent of the thin wall setting.

description: Creates a thin-walled lofted boss (protrusion) feature where the wall extends in the reverse direction (opposite to the loft's natural direction) from the loft centerline surface. When ThinType=1 (swThinWallOppDirection), the Thickness1 parameter specifies the wall thickness in the reverse direction, creating material on the opposite side compared to ThinType=0. The feature interpolates through multiple closed cross-sectional profiles with optional guide curves and centerline.

description: This feature case represents a boss loft with thin wall properties configured for mid-plane (symmetric) thickness distribution. Created using InsertProtrusionBlend2() with IsThinBody=true and ThinType=2. The loft constructs a protruding feature from multiple profiles with optional guide curves and centerline. Mid-plane configuration creates thin walls with thickness distributed symmetrically about the loft surface. Note: The exact behavior of Thickness1/Thickness2 parameters for mid-plane mode is not explicitly documented in the API; additional testing may be required to confirm whether these values are used or if SetWallThickness() must be called post-creation.

description: Creates a lofted protrusion (boss) feature with thin wall properties applied in two directions. When IsThinBody is true and ThinType parameter is set to 3 (swThinWallTwoDirection), the loft creates a thin-walled solid where material thickness extends in both directions from the loft surface. Thickness1 controls the wall thickness in the forward direction, and Thickness2 controls thickness in the reverse direction, allowing asymmetric wall thicknesses.

description: Creates a lofted protrusion (boss) feature using multiple cross-sectional profiles with optional guide curves and centerline in a multibody part, where the feature scope is restricted to affect only selected bodies (UseFeatScope=true). When combined with UseAutoSelect=false, you must pre-select the target solid bodies BEFORE calling InsertProtrusionBlend2; when combined with UseAutoSelect=true, all intersecting bodies are automatically affected.

description: Creates a lofted protrusion (boss) feature with Merge=true, which merges the resulting loft body with existing solid bodies in a multibody part. The feature lofts through multiple cross-sectional profiles with optional guide curves and centerline. When Merge=true, the new loft body is merged with all intersecting solid bodies (subject to UseFeatureScope and UseAutoSelect settings), creating a unified solid body. If no existing bodies are present, the loft creates the first body in the part.

This can be configured either during creation via InsertProtrusionBlend2's ForceNonRational parameter, or after creation by modifying the ILoftFeatureData.AdvancedSmoothing property via GetDefinition()/AccessSelections()/ModifyDefinition() workflow.

description: Creates a lofted protrusion (boss) feature using guide curves with controlled influence type. The GuideCurveInfluence parameter specifies how guide curves shape the lofted surface, with options for local (NextGuide), sharp edge (NextSharp), edge-bounded (NextEdge), or global influence (NextGlobal). Multiple guide curves require specific compound mark values during selection (4098, 8194, 12290, 16386) rather than simple sequential marks.

description: A lofted cut feature created from multiple cross-sectional profiles without guide curves or centerline. Profiles must be selected in order using SelectByID2 with mark=1 before calling InsertCutBlend(). The method interpolates between profiles to create a complex cavity. Note: Cut features resulting in multiple bodies cannot preserve all resulting bodies.

description: A lofted cut feature creates a cavity by blending through multiple cross-sectional profiles. Profiles, guide curves (optional), and centerline (optional) must be pre-selected with specific marks before calling InsertCutBlend. The feature supports tangency constraints at start/end profiles through StartMatchingType/EndMatchingType parameters, which require additional selections (tangency vectors or faces) when using values other than 0. Unlike InsertProtrusionBlend2, InsertCutBlend does not support the GuideCurveInfluence parameter or tangent length/direction controls.

description: Creates a lofted cut (cavity) feature by blending through multiple cross-sectional profiles with optional guide curves and centerline. When Close=true, the loft creates a closed surface that connects the last profile back to the first, which is then subtracted from the solid body. IMPORTANT: For closed cut lofts, if fewer than 3 profiles are selected, any guide curves used must be closed curves.

description: Creates a lofted cut feature with optional tangency constraints applied at the start and/or end profile to control surface continuity and shape. Profile selection order must be consistent with the desired loft direction. Guide curves are strongly recommended, especially when selecting profiles from the FeatureManager design tree. When cut features result in multiple bodies, you cannot select to keep specific resulting bodies.

Critical limitation: When cut features result in multiple bodies, you cannot select to keep all or only selected resulting bodies.

description: Modifies an existing loft feature by retrieving its definition via IFeature.GetDefinition(), modifying properties like profiles, guide curves, centerline, and pick points, then applying changes via IFeature.ModifyDefinition(). The workflow requires calling AccessSelections() before modifying selection-dependent properties (Profiles, GuideCurves, Centerline, PickPoints) and either ModifyDefinition() or ReleaseSelectionAccess() to restore rollback state. If ModifyDefinition() returns false, check IFeature.GetErrorCode2() for rebuild errors.

description: This use case covers modifying an existing loft feature's endpoint tangency constraints by adjusting tangency type properties (StartTangencyType, EndTangencyType) or tangent length properties (StartTangentLength, EndTangentLength) through the IFeature.ModifyDefinition() workflow. For simple tangency changes (None, NormalToProfile), call GetDefinition(), modify properties, and call ModifyDefinition(). For tangency types requiring selections (DirectionVector, AllFaces), call AccessSelections() first to set the selection references, then ModifyDefinition() to apply changes.

description: Modify an existing loft feature's thin wall configuration by changing the wall type and thickness values. This workflow applies only to loft features that are already thin features (verify with IsThinFeature()). Unlike modifying selections, changing thin wall properties does not require AccessSelections() - simply get the definition, modify the ThinWallType property and/or call SetWallThickness, then apply changes with ModifyDefinition().

description: Modifying an existing loft feature to change feature scope bodies in multibody parts requires getting the feature definition via GetDefinition(), calling AccessSelections() to enable modification, adjusting FeatureScope (enables/disables body scoping), AutoSelect (auto vs. manual body selection - only valid when FeatureScope is true), and FeatureScopeBodies (array of IBody2 objects), then applying changes via ModifyDefinition(). When FeatureScope is false, the loft affects all bodies regardless of FeatureScopeBodies. When FeatureScope is true, the loft expands along its creation plane and affects only bodies it intersects from the FeatureScopeBodies array (if AutoSelect is false) or all intersecting bodies (if AutoSelect is true). ModifyDefinition() automatically restores rollback state, so ReleaseSelectionAccess() should only be used when modifications are abandoned.

description: Creates an edge chamfer feature with two distances on each side of selected edges using InsertFeatureChamfer. This method accepts pre-selected edges and chamfer parameters in a single call. The swChamferType_e enum values can be bitwise OR'd: use swChamferDistanceDistance (2) for unequal distances on each side, or combine it with swChamferEqualDistance (16) to get type 18 for equal distances. For type 2, specify VertexChamDist1 and VertexChamDist2 individually; for type 18, use the OtherDist parameter for a single distance value applied to both sides.

description: Creates an edge chamfer with equal distance on both sides using IFeatureManager::InsertFeatureChamfer. This method directly inserts a chamfer feature on pre-selected edges by accepting ChamferType=swChamferEqualDistance (16) and passing the distance value via the OtherDist parameter. The resulting feature has Type=2 (swChamferDistanceDistance) with EqualDistance=true, applying the specified distance symmetrically on both sides of each selected edge. The method returns an IFeature object for accessing the feature definition.

description: A vertex chamfer is created by selecting a single vertex with exactly three adjacent edges of the same convexity and applying three independent distance values (one for each edge in the order returned by IVertex::EnumEdgesOriented). This is accomplished using IFeatureManager::InsertFeatureChamfer with chamfer type swChamferVertex (value 3), passing three distance parameters (VertexChamDist1, VertexChamDist2, VertexChamDist3) corresponding to the three edges. For non-linear edges, distance values represent arc length, not chordal distance. The method returns an IFeature object if successful, or null if the vertex doesn't meet the requirements.

description: Creates a constant radius chamfer feature with partial edge parameters using ISimpleFilletFeatureData2. This feature is created by initializing a constant radius fillet (swConstRadiusFillet) and specializing it to a chamfer via ConicTypeForCrossSectionProfile set to swFeatureFilletConicRhoZeroChamfer.
