description: Modifying an existing chamfer feature by retrieving its IChamferFeatureData2 object via IFeature::GetDefinition, adjusting its properties (Type, EdgeChamferAngle, edge/vertex distances, geometric selections, etc.), and applying changes through IFeature::ModifyDefinition. This workflow allows programmatic modification of chamfer parameters. Important: Always call IFeature::GetErrorCode2 after ModifyDefinition to verify successful modification, even if ModifyDefinition returns true. Use AccessSelections before reading/modifying geometric entities (Edges, Faces, Loops, Vertex), then call either ModifyDefinition to apply changes or ReleaseSelectionAccess to cancel. For assemblies, call IAssemblyDoc::EditAssembly after modification to exit Editing Part mode.

example_code_snippets:
```csharp
// Example 1: Basic 10mm diameter, 25mm deep single-ended blind hole
var featureManager = modelDoc.FeatureManager;
Feature holeFeat = featureManager.SimpleHole2(
    0.01,                      // 10mm diameter
    true,                      // Single-ended
    false,                     // Normal cut direction
    false,                     // Normal extrusion direction
    (int)swEndCondBlind,       // Blind end condition
    0,                         // T2 ignored
    0.025,                     // 25mm depth
    0.0,                       // D2 ignored
    false, false,              // No draft angles
    false, false,              // Draft direction ignored
    0.0, 0.0,                  // Draft angles ignored
    false, false,              // Offset parameters not applicable for blind
    false, false,              // Surface translation not applicable for blind
    true, true,                // Feature scope: auto-select all bodies
    false, false, false        // Part feature (not assembly)
);

// Example 2: Single-ended blind hole with reversed cut direction
// (hole cuts in opposite direction from sketch normal)
Feature holeFeat2 = featureManager.SimpleHole2(
    0.008,                     // 8mm diameter
    true,                      // Single-ended
    true,                      // Reverse cut direction
    false,                     // Normal extrusion
    (int)swEndCondBlind,
    0, 0.015, 0.0,             // 15mm blind depth
    false, false, false, false, 0.0, 0.0,
    false, false, false, false,
    true, true, false, false, false
);

// Example 3: Single-ended blind hole with draft angle for manufacturing
// 5-degree draft (0.0873 radians) outward
Feature holeFeat3 = featureManager.SimpleHole2(
    0.012,                     // 12mm diameter
    true,                      // Single-ended
    false, false,
    (int)swEndCondBlind,
    0, 0.030, 0.0,             // 30mm blind depth
    true,                      // Enable draft on first end
    false,                     // Dchk2 ignored
    false,                     // Draft outward
    false,                     // Ddir2 ignored
    0.0873,                    // 5-degree draft angle in radians
    0.0,                       // Dang2 ignored
    false, false, false, false,
    true, true, false, false, false
);

// Example 4: Modify hole properties after creation using feature data
var holeDef = (ISimpleHoleFeatureData2)holeFeat.GetDefinition();
holeDef.AccessSelections(modelDoc, null);

holeDef.Diameter = 0.015;              // Change to 15mm diameter
holeDef.Depth = 0.040;                 // Change to 40mm depth
holeDef.Type = (int)swEndCondBlind;    // Ensure blind end condition
holeDef.ReverseDirection = false;      // Ensure normal cut direction
holeDef.DraftWhileExtruding = true;    // Enable draft
holeDef.DraftAngle = 0.0524;           // 3-degree draft (in radians)
holeDef.DraftOutward = false;          // Draft outward

holeFeat.ModifyDefinition(holeDef, modelDoc, null);
```

Uses swEndCondThroughAll end condition which ignores depth values and geometric references, making it the simplest hole configuration for creating fully penetrating holes.

description: This feature instance creates a single-ended simple hole with an offset-from-surface end condition, where the hole depth is specified as an offset distance from a reference surface or plane. CRITICAL: When using SimpleHole2 to create this hole type, the reference surface must be pre-selected before calling the method, as SimpleHole2 does not have a parameter to specify it. Alternatively, create the feature with a different end condition then modify it via ISimpleHoleFeatureData2 to set the offset-from-surface end condition and reference using SetEndConditionReference().

description: Creates a single-ended simple hole feature with blind end condition and optional draft angle. This feature case uses Sd=true for single-ended operation, T1=swEndCondBlind for blind termination, and Dchk1=true to enable drafting. The draft direction (Ddir1) controls whether the hole tapers inward (true) or outward (false), with the draft angle specified via Dang1. When creating single-ended holes, all second-end parameters (T2, D2, Dchk2, Ddir2, Dang2, OffsetReverse2, TranslateSurface2) are ignored by the API.

// Example 1: Single-ended blind hole with 5-degree inward draft
// Units assumption: Draft angle likely in degrees (not explicitly documented)

SelectionMgr swSelMgr = swModel.SelectionManager;
FeatureManager swFeatMgr = swModel.FeatureManager;

// ... Select face and setup sketch point ...

// Create hole: 1mm diameter, 5mm deep, 5° inward draft
Feature holeFeat = swFeatMgr.SimpleHole2(
    0.001,         // 1mm diameter
    true,          // Single-ended
    false,         // No direction flip
    false,         // No extrude flip
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,  // Blind end condition
    0,             // Ignored (second end)
    0.005,         // 5mm depth
    0.0,           // Ignored (second end)
    true,          // Enable draft angle
    false,         // Ignored (second end)
    true,          // Inward draft (true = inward, false = outward)
    false,         // Ignored (second end)
    5.0,           // 5-degree draft (assuming degrees)
    0.0,           // Ignored (second end)
    false,         // No offset reverse
    false,         // Ignored (second end)
    false,         // No surface translate
    false,         // Ignored (second end)
    true,          // Use feature scope
    true,          // Auto-select bodies
    false,         // No assembly scope
    false,         // No auto-select components
    false          // No propagate to parts
);

// Example 2: Single-ended blind hole with outward draft
Feature holeFeat2 = swFeatMgr.SimpleHole2(
    0.002,         // 2mm diameter
    true,          // Single-ended
    false, false,
    0,             // swEndCondBlind
    0,             // Ignored
    0.010,         // 10mm depth
    0.0,           // Ignored
    true,          // Enable draft
    false,         // Ignored
    false,         // Outward draft (false = outward)
    false,         // Ignored
    3.0,           // 3-degree draft
    0.0,           // Ignored
    false, false, false, false,
    true, true, false, false, false
);

description: Creates a double-ended simple hole feature with independent end conditions specified at creation time. The SimpleHole2 method accepts separate parameters for each end (T1/T2 for end condition types, D1/D2 for depths, Dchk1/Dchk2, Ddir1/Ddir2, Dang1/Dang2 for draft angles, OffsetReverse1/OffsetReverse2 and TranslateSurface1/TranslateSurface2 for offset behavior). However, the ISimpleHoleFeatureData2 interface has only one set of properties (Type, Depth, Face, DraftAngle, etc.) which appear to control only one end - modification of both ends independently after creation is not directly supported through the feature data interface.

This feature supports various end conditions, configurable depth and diameter, and draft angles. Assembly-related properties are not applicable to part documents.

description: Creates a simple hole feature in a multibody part using automatic body selection. This feature requires UseFeatScope=true to enable the UseAutoSelect=true parameter, which automatically selects all bodies affected by the hole. The hole geometry supports various configurations including diameter, end conditions (blind, through-all, up-to-selection, offset-from-surface), draft angles, and direction settings. Note that depth property only applies to blind holes; other end conditions require alternative methods to query depth.

example_code_snippets:
```csharp
// Example 1: Creating a simple through-all hole with auto-select in multibody part
// PREREQUISITE: UseFeatScope MUST be true for UseAutoSelect to work
FeatureManager swFeatMgr = swModel.FeatureManager;

// Create hole: diameter=0.001m, single-ended, through-all, auto-select all bodies
Feature holeFeat = swFeatMgr.SimpleHole2(
    0.001,  // Dia: 1mm diameter
    true,   // Sd: single-ended
    false,  // Flip: don't flip cut direction
    false,  // Dir: don't flip extrude direction
    1,      // T1: swEndCondThroughAll
    0,      // T2: ignored for single-ended
    0.001,  // D1: ignored for through-all end condition
    0.001,  // D2: not used (single-ended)
    false,  // Dchk1: no draft angle
    false,  // Dchk2: no draft angle
    false, false, // Ddir1, Ddir2: N/A (no draft)
    0, 0,   // Dang1, Dang2: N/A (no draft)
    false, false, // OffsetReverse1/2: N/A (not offset condition)
    false, false, // TranslateSurface1/2: N/A (not offset condition)
    true,   // UseFeatScope: MUST be true for auto-select
    true,   // UseAutoSelect: AUTO-SELECT ALL BODIES (requires UseFeatScope=true)
    false, false, false // Assembly params: N/A for part documents
);

// Example 2: Blind hole with manual body selection (UseAutoSelect=false)
Feature manualHole = swFeatMgr.SimpleHole2(
    0.0015, // Dia: 1.5mm diameter
    true,   // Sd: single-ended
    false, false,
    0,      // T1: swEndCondBlind
    0, 0.002, 0, // T2, D1: 2mm depth, D2
    false, false, false, false, 0, 0,
    false, false, false, false,
    true,   // UseFeatScope: true to enable body scoping
    false,  // UseAutoSelect: FALSE - manual body selection required
    false, false, false
);

// For manual selection, must specify bodies after creation:
SimpleHoleFeatureData2 holeData = (SimpleHoleFeatureData2)manualHole.GetDefinition();
holeData.AccessSelections(swModel, null);
// ... select specific bodies using ISetFeatureScopeBodies ...
holeData.ReleaseSelectionAccess();

// Example 3: Hole with offset-from-surface end condition
Feature offsetHole = swFeatMgr.SimpleHole2(
    0.001,  // Dia
    true,   // Sd: single-ended
    false, false,
    5,      // T1: swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
    0, 0.001, 0,
    true,   // Dchk1: ENABLE draft angle
    false,  // Dchk2: N/A (single-ended)
    true,   // Ddir1: inward draft
    false,  // Ddir2: N/A
    5.0,    // Dang1: draft angle (units unclear, likely radians)
    0,      // Dang2: N/A
    false,  // OffsetReverse1: offset toward sketch
    false,  // OffsetReverse2: N/A
    false,  // TranslateSurface1: FALSE for true offset (per documentation)
    false,  // TranslateSurface2: N/A
    true,   // UseFeatScope: true
    true,   // UseAutoSelect: AUTO-SELECT
    false, false, false
);

// IMPORTANT: Depth property limitations
// - Only works for blind holes
// - For through-all/other conditions, depth is undefined or requires alternative methods
```

description: Creates a simple hole feature in an assembly with scope and component selection options. This feature allows specifying hole diameter, end conditions (blind, through-all, offset from surface, etc.), depths, draft angles, and assembly-specific scope settings. The AssemblyFeatureScope parameter controls whether the hole affects all components (false) or only selected components (true). AutoSelectComponents determines if all affected components are automatically selected (true) or only manually pre-selected components are used (false). PropagateFeatureToParts controls whether the hole is propagated to individual part files (true) or remains an assembly-level feature (false).

description: Modifying an existing simple hole feature by retrieving its definition via IFeature::GetDefinition, updating properties on ISimpleHoleFeatureData2 (such as Depth for blind holes, Type for end conditions, Diameter, draft parameters, and end condition references), then applying changes with IFeature::ModifyDefinition. The workflow requires calling AccessSelections to enter a rollback state before modifications, then either ModifyDefinition to commit changes or ReleaseSelectionAccess to abort. Note that Depth property only supports blind holes; other end conditions use alternative properties or references.

description: Creates a constraint-based reference plane at a specified distance offset from a single selected reference plane. The plane is created using IFeatureManager::InsertRefPlane with a Distance constraint (8) and distance value in meters. The offset direction can be reversed using the OptionFlip flag (256) combined with the Distance constraint via bitwise OR. Before calling InsertRefPlane, select the reference plane using SelectByID2 with mark 0.

The plane is created at the specified angle relative to the base reference, rotating around the edge.

description: Creates a constraint-based reference plane using the IFeatureManager::InsertRefPlane method with explicitly selected reference entities and geometric constraints (parallel, perpendicular, angle, distance, etc.). This feature case specifically demonstrates parallel constraint application. Reference planes created via this method (SOLIDWORKS 2010+) are constraint-based and automatically sized to model geometry. The created plane is not automatically selected after creation, and display visibility is controlled by document-level swDisplayPlanes preference.

Constraints define spatial relationships (coincident, parallel, perpendicular, angle, distance, tangent) between the plane and existing geometry. For coincident constraint, the plane is made coplanar with the selected face or plane entity. Visibility is controlled by the swDisplayPlanes user preference.

Valid reference entities include planar faces, reference planes, and other planar surfaces.

description: Creates a constraint-based reference plane with projection options for on-surface planes using IFeatureManager::InsertRefPlane. This feature case specifically addresses on-surface reference planes that project geometry onto a surface, with the option to project either along the sketch normal (perpendicular to the active sketch plane) or to the nearest location on the reference surface. Projection options are constraint modifiers used with surface selections to control plane orientation.

description: Creates a constraint-based reference plane using selected reference entities with constraint types defined by bitmask flags from swRefPlaneReferenceConstraints_e. Constraints must be combined using bitwise OR operations. The OptionProjectToNearestLocation flag (0x400 equals 1024 decimal) projects to the nearest location on the reference surface. The method accepts up to three constraint-value pairs and returns an IRefPlane object. Available in SOLIDWORKS 2010 and later.

description: This feature case creates a constraint-based reference plane with the option to place its origin on a curve (edge) rather than at a vertex or point. When creating the plane using InsertRefPlane, the OriginOnCurve option can be specified by OR'ing swRefPlaneReferenceConstraint_OptionOriginOnCurve (512 or 0x200) with the primary constraint type. For example, combining an angle constraint (16) with the OriginOnCurve option: 16 | 512 = 528. After creation, the plane's origin placement can be verified or modified via the IRefPlaneFeatureData::OriginOnCurve property. This feature is particularly useful when creating planes perpendicular to curves or edges where precise origin positioning along the curve is required, replacing the functionality previously provided by the obsolete CreatePlanePerCurveAndPassPoint3 method's OrigAtCurve parameter.

description: Creates a constraint-based reference plane using IFeatureManager::InsertRefPlane by specifying constraint types (angle, distance, parallel, perpendicular, etc.) for up to three reference entities. Supports reversing reference direction/orientation during creation using the swRefPlaneReferenceConstraint_OptionReferenceFlip (0x2000) constraint flag, which is ORed with the primary constraint type. This is distinct from the IRefPlaneFeatureData::ReversedReferenceDirection property used to modify direction after plane creation.

To maintain parallel-to-screen orientation when the view changes, use IRefPlaneFeatureData::UpdatePlane property via Feature::ModifyDefinition after selecting the plane.

description: Retrieves and modifies reference plane feature properties via IFeature::GetDefinition, allowing changes to constraints, distances, angles of both constraint-based (SOLIDWORKS 2010+) and non-constraint-based (SOLIDWORKS 2009 or earlier) reference planes. For constraint-based planes, properties like Distance, Angle, and AutoSize can be modified. For non-constraint-based planes, indexed properties Reference(Index), Constraint(Index), and AngleOrDistance(Index) can be modified using swRefPlaneReferenceIndex_e enum values.

description: This feature case documents how to modify an existing reference axis feature that is defined by a single line. It covers retrieving the feature definition using IFeature::GetDefinition(), accessing its selections, modifying the Type property to swAxisTypeOneLine (value 0), and updating the selections with a new line entity. For swAxisTypeOneLine, exactly one linear entity is required, which can be an edge (IEdge), a sketch line segment (ISketchSegment), another axis feature (IRefAxis), or similar linear geometric entities. The GetSelections method returns both the entity objects and their swSelectType_e enum values.

description: A reference axis created by defining two planes whose intersection line becomes the axis. This use case demonstrates modifying an existing reference axis feature by changing its Type property to swAxisTypeTwoPlanes (value 1) and providing exactly two plane entity selections. The workflow requires calling AccessSelections to enter rollback mode, setting the Type property and two plane selections via SetSelections, then calling ModifyDefinition to apply changes (which also exits rollback mode).

description: This feature case covers modifying a reference axis feature to be defined by two points. It involves retrieving the feature definition from an existing reference axis feature, setting its Type property to swAxisTypeTwoPoints (value 2), and providing two point entities as selections. Valid point entity types include vertices (swSelVERTICES), datum/reference points (swSelDATUMPOINTS), sketch points (swSelSKETCHPOINTS), or external sketch points (swSelEXTSKETCHPOINTS). The modifications are then applied to the feature using ModifyDefinition.

description: This feature case allows you to modify an existing reference axis feature to use a cylindrical or conical face as its defining geometry. By retrieving the feature data definition from an existing reference axis feature, you can update its Type property to swAxisTypeCylOrConeFace (value 3) and set its selection to a single cylindrical or conical face. The axis will be created along the centerline of the cylindrical or conical surface.

description: A reference axis defined by a point and a plane is created by setting the Type property to swAxisTypePtAndPlane (4) and providing two selections: a point entity (vertex or datum point) and a plane entity (planar face or reference plane). The order of selections in the array is [0] = point, [1] = plane. The resulting axis passes through the specified point with its orientation defined by the normal of the selected plane.

description: A single direction linear pattern feature that replicates geometry along Direction 1 with user-specified spacing and instance count. This is the most basic form of linear pattern where instances are evenly distributed along a selected direction with fixed spacing distance. Requires pre-selection of direction geometry (marked 1) and entities to pattern (marked 4 for features, 256 for bodies) with ordered selection when using SelectByID2.

description: Creates a single direction linear pattern feature with an up-to reference geometry constraint in Direction 1. Instead of specifying fixed spacing and instance count, the pattern extends from the seed instance toward a selected reference geometry (face, plane, edge, or vertex) with an optional offset. The pattern automatically calculates spacing and instance distribution based on the up-to reference. Critical pre-selection with specific marks (2097152 for up-to reference, 8388608 for seed reference if used) must be performed before calling CreateDefinition.

description: Creates a bidirectional linear pattern feature where instances are replicated in both Direction 1 and Direction 2 using spacing and instance counts (swPatternEndCondition_SpacingAndInstances mode). The pattern replicates the seed feature or body along perpendicular directions with configurable spacing between instances and total instance counts in each direction. Direction 2 can optionally pattern only the seed feature without replicating Direction 1 instances using D2PatternSeedOnly property.

description: Creates a bidirectional linear pattern where Direction 1 uses spacing and instance count (swPatternEndCondition_SpacingAndInstances) while Direction 2 extends to an up-to reference geometry (swPatternEndCondition_UpToReference). Direction 2 requires selecting both a direction axis and an up-to reference geometry, with configurable offset behavior. The D2EndUseSpacing property controls whether spacing or instance count determines pattern density up to the reference. When D2EndUseSeedReference is true, D2EndSeedReference must specify which seed geometry to measure offsets from; otherwise offsets are measured from the centroid.

description: Creates a bidirectional linear pattern feature where pattern instances in both Direction 1 and Direction 2 are controlled by up-to reference geometry with specified offsets. This variant requires pre-selecting two up-to reference entities (one for each direction) along with the seed feature and direction axes before creating the feature data object. The pattern extends from the seed feature up to each specified reference geometry with optional offsets measured from either the centroid or specific seed geometry.

description: A swept-boss feature created by defining a sketch profile and sweep path, then configuring the sweep to extend in both directions along the path. The profile must be a sketch, and the path must extend through both sides of the profile. Bidirectional sweeps allow independent twist control in each direction. When using constant twist angles, AlignWithEndFaces must be set to false, and StartTangencyType and EndTangencyType properties are not valid.

description: A swept-cut feature with sketch profile removes material by sweeping a 2D sketch profile along a path. The profile and path must be pre-selected using SelectByID2 before creating the feature definition. Optional guide curves can constrain the sweep. The feature supports twist control, tangency settings, and thin-wall options. Critical constraints include: PathAlignmentType only valid when TwistControlType=0, AlignWithEndFaces must be false with constant twist, guide curves invalid with bidirectional sweeps, and ThinFeature must be set before CreateFeature.

specialized_signature:
' Swept-cut with sketch profile
' CRITICAL: Pre-select profile and path BEFORE CreateDefinition

boolstatus = docExt.SelectByID2("Sketch1", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, False, 1, Nothing, 0)
boolstatus = docExt.SelectByID2("Sketch2", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, True, 4, Nothing, 0)
' Optional guide curves: mark = 2

Set swSweep = featMgr.CreateDefinition(swFmSweepCut)

' READ-ONLY after AccessSelections:
' swSweep.Profile - IFace2/IEdge/ICurve
' swSweep.Path - ICurve/IEdge
' swSweep.GuideCurves - Array

swSweep.CircularProfile = False
swSweep.TwistControlType = 0  ' 0=FollowPath, 1=KeepNormal, 8=ConstantTwist
swSweep.PathAlignmentType = 0  ' Only valid when TwistControlType=0
swSweep.StartTangencyType = 0  ' Not valid if Direction=1 or TwistControlType=8
swSweep.EndTangencyType = 0
swSweep.AlignWithEndFaces = True  ' Must be False if TwistControlType=8
swSweep.MergeSmoothFaces = True
swSweep.FeatureScope = True
swSweep.AutoSelect = True
' Do NOT use Merge property (boss only)
swSweep.ThinFeature = False  ' Must set before CreateFeature
swSweep.ThinWallType = 0
swSweep.SetWallThickness True, 0.0
swSweep.SetTwistAngle 0.0  ' Only when TwistControlType=8
swSweep.TangentPropagation = False
swSweep.AdvancedSmoothing = False
swSweep.MaintainTangency = False
swSweep.Direction = 0  ' Guide curves invalid if =1

Set feat = featMgr.CreateFeature(swSweep)

Set swSweep = feat.GetDefinition
swSweep.AccessSelections model, Nothing
' Access Profile, Path, GuideCurves
swSweep.ReleaseSelectionAccess
example_code_snippets:
' Example: Basic swept-cut
Dim Part As ModelDoc2
Dim swSweep As SweepFeatureData
Dim feat As Feature

Set Part = swApp.ActiveDoc
Part.ClearSelection2 True
Part.Extension.SelectByID2 "Sketch2", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, False, 1, Nothing, 0
Part.Extension.SelectByID2 "Sketch3", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, True, 4, Nothing, 0

Set swSweep = Part.FeatureManager.CreateDefinition(swFmSweepCut)
swSweep.TwistControlType = 0
swSweep.PathAlignmentType = 0
swSweep.CircularProfile = False
swSweep.FeatureScope = True
swSweep.AutoSelect = True

Set feat = Part.FeatureManager.CreateFeature(swSweep)

Set swSweep = feat.GetDefinition
swSweep.AccessSelections Part, Nothing
cutType = swSweep.GetCutSweepOption()
swSweep.ReleaseSelectionAccess

' Example: Constant twist (requires AlignWithEndFaces=False)
Part.Extension.SelectByID2 "Sketch2", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, False, 1, Nothing, 0
Part.Extension.SelectByID2 "Sketch3", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, True, 4, Nothing, 0

Set swSweep = Part.FeatureManager.CreateDefinition(swFmSweepCut)
swSweep.TwistControlType = 8
swSweep.AlignWithEndFaces = False
swSweep.SetTwistAngle 0.785
Set feat = Part.FeatureManager.CreateFeature(swSweep)

description: A swept-cut feature using a circular profile removes material by sweeping a circular cross-section along a selected path. Instead of using a sketch-based profile, this feature uses a circular profile with a specified diameter, making it ideal for creating holes, slots, and other features with consistent circular cross-sections along curved or complex paths. The path must be selected before creating the feature, and the circular profile is controlled via CircularProfile and CircularProfileDiameter properties.

description: This feature case documents how to create a swept-cut feature using a tool body (solid sweep). The tool body is a solid feature (typically a revolve or cylindrical extrude) that sweeps along a path to remove material from a target body. Unlike sketch-based sweeps, tool bodies must meet specific geometric constraints: they must be convex, not merged with the main body, and consist of either a revolved feature with analytical geometry only (lines and arcs) or a cylindrical extruded feature. This functionality requires SOLIDWORKS API 2017 FCS or later. Selection order and marks are critical: tool body (mark 1), path (mark 4), and target body (mark 2048).

description: A swept-cut thin-walled feature with 1D wall thickness creates a thin-walled cut by sweeping a profile along a path and removing material in a single direction. This feature requires ISweepFeatureData with ThinFeature set to true BEFORE calling CreateFeature (this cannot be changed after feature creation). For 1D wall thickness, set ThinWallType to 0 (swThinWallOneDirection - applies thickness in Direction 1) or 1 (swThinWallOppDirection - applies thickness in Direction 2/reverse). Use SetWallThickness to specify the thickness value; which direction(s) are applied depends on the ThinWallType setting.

specialized_signature:
' Create swept-cut thin-walled feature with 1D wall thickness
' IMPORTANT: ThinFeature must be set to True BEFORE calling CreateFeature
' and cannot be changed after feature creation

Set obj = featureMgr.CreateDefinition(swFmSweepCut)

' Profile and path selection (MUST be done via SelectByID2 before CreateDefinition)
' Profile: select sketch or 2D geometry (selection mark 1)
' Path: select sketch or curve geometry (selection mark 4)

' Core thin-walling properties - CRITICAL ORDER
obj.ThinFeature = True                          ' MUST be set before CreateFeature; cannot be changed after
obj.ThinWallType = 0                            ' 0=swThinWallOneDirection (uses Direction 1 thickness)
                                                ' 1=swThinWallOppDirection (uses Direction 2 thickness)
                                                ' For 1D wall thickness, use 0 or 1
obj.SetWallThickness True, 0.001                ' Direction 1 thickness in meters (used if ThinWallType=0)
obj.SetWallThickness False, 0.001               ' Direction 2 thickness in meters (used if ThinWallType=1)
                                                ' Note: SetWallThickness behavior depends on ThinWallType

' Sweep profile orientation control
obj.TwistControlType = 0                        ' swTwistControlType_e: 0=FollowPath, 1=KeepNormalConstant, 8=ConstantTwistAlongPath, etc.
obj.PathAlignmentType = 0                       ' swTangencyType_e: 0=None, 2=DirectionVector, 3=AllFaces, 10=MinimumTwist
obj.CircularProfile = False                     ' True if using circular profile without sketch; False for sketch profile
obj.CircularProfileDiameter = 0.0               ' Diameter if CircularProfile is True

' Alignment and smoothing options
obj.AlignWithEndFaces = True                    ' Align sweep with end faces if available
obj.MaintainTangency = False                    ' Merge tangent faces
obj.AdvancedSmoothing = False                   ' Apply advanced smoothing
obj.MergeSmoothFaces = True                     ' Merge smooth faces when using guide curves
obj.TangentPropagation = False                  ' Propagate to next tangent edge

' Tangency at path endpoints
obj.StartTangencyType = 0                       ' swTangencyType_e: 0=None, 1=NormalToProfile
obj.EndTangencyType = 0                         ' swTangencyType_e: 0=None, 1=NormalToProfile
obj.SetTwistAngle 0.0                           ' Twist angle in radians along path

' Body and scope management
obj.FeatureScope = True                         ' Use scope in multibody part
obj.AutoSelect = True                           ' Automatically select all bodies in multibody part
obj.Direction = 0                               ' swSweepDirection_e: 0=Direction1, 1=Bidirectional, 2=Direction2
                                                ' NOTE: Only valid if profile is sketch AND path extends through both sides

' Assembly-specific properties (for assembly cut-sweep features)
obj.AssemblyFeatureScope = True                 ' Feature scope for assembly components
obj.AutoSelectComponents = True                 ' Auto-select assembly components
obj.PropagateFeatureToParts = False             ' Extend feature to affected parts

' Feature scope bodies (if FeatureScope=True and AutoSelect=False)
obj.FeatureScopeBodies = [...]                  ' Array of Body2 objects to affect

' IGNORED/NOT APPLICABLE for Cut Sweeps:
' - Merge: not valid for sweep-cut features (only for boss sweeps)
' - IsBossFeature: read-only property, determined by swFmSweepCut vs swFmSweep
' - Profile, Path: accessed via GetDefinition after AccessSelections, not directly settable
' - GuideCurves: optional array for guide curve control (requires separate selection)
example_code_snippets:
'--------------------------------------------------------------------
' Example: Create a swept-cut thin-walled feature with 1D wall thickness
' using ThinWallType = 0 (One Direction)
'--------------------------------------------------------------------
Dim swApp As SldWorks.SldWorks
Dim swModel As SldWorks.ModelDoc2
Dim swFeatureMgr As SldWorks.FeatureManager
Dim swSweep As SldWorks.SweepFeatureData
Dim swFeature As SldWorks.Feature
Dim status As Boolean

Set swApp = Application.SldWorks
Set swModel = swApp.ActiveDoc
Set swFeatureMgr = swModel.FeatureManager

' STEP 1: Select profile and path BEFORE creating definition
' Profile must be sketch or 2D geometry (mark=1)
' Path must be sketch or curve geometry (mark=4)
status = swModel.Extension.SelectByID2("Sketch2", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, False, 1, Nothing, 0)
status = swModel.Extension.SelectByID2("Sketch3", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, True, 4, Nothing, 0)

' STEP 2: Create sweep-cut definition
Set swSweep = swFeatureMgr.CreateDefinition(swFmSweepCut)

' STEP 3: Configure for thin-walled operation with 1D thickness
' CRITICAL: ThinFeature must be set BEFORE CreateFeature
swSweep.ThinFeature = True
swSweep.ThinWallType = 0                        ' One Direction (uses Direction 1)
swSweep.SetWallThickness True, 0.001            ' 1mm thickness in Direction 1 (meters)
swSweep.SetWallThickness False, 0.0             ' Direction 2 not used for ThinWallType=0

' STEP 4: Configure sweep behavior
swSweep.TwistControlType = 0
swSweep.PathAlignmentType = 0
swSweep.CircularProfile = False
swSweep.AlignWithEndFaces = True
swSweep.MaintainTangency = False
swSweep.AdvancedSmoothing = False
swSweep.StartTangencyType = 0
swSweep.EndTangencyType = 0
swSweep.SetTwistAngle 0#
swSweep.MergeSmoothFaces = True
swSweep.TangentPropagation = False
swSweep.FeatureScope = True
swSweep.AutoSelect = True

' STEP 5: Create the feature
Set swFeature = swFeatureMgr.CreateFeature(swSweep)

' STEP 6: Verify and access properties
If Not swFeature Is Nothing Then
    Debug.Print "Thin-walled sweep-cut created: " & swFeature.Name
    Set swSweep = swFeature.GetDefinition
    status = swSweep.AccessSelections(swModel, Nothing)
    Debug.Print "Is thin-walled: " & swSweep.IsThinFeature
    Debug.Print "Thin wall type: " & swSweep.ThinWallType
    Debug.Print "Wall thickness Direction 1: " & swSweep.GetWallThickness(True) * 1000 & " mm"
    swSweep.ReleaseSelectionAccess
End If

description: A swept-cut thin-walled feature with 1D-reverse (opposite direction) wall thickness applies a sweep operation to remove material from a solid body while maintaining uniform wall thickness in the opposite direction from the profile's normal. The wall extends in Direction 2 (reverse/opposite direction) only, creating a hollow cavity with a consistent wall on one side. This configuration uses ThinWallType=1 (swThinWallOppDirection).

description: Swept-cut thin-walled features with mid-plane wall thickness create a swept cut with uniform wall thickness extending equally in both directions from the sweep profile centerline. This uses swThinWallMidPlane mode (ThinWallType = 2), which creates true symmetric wall geometry around the profile, useful for creating hollow swept cuts like channels and tubes with perfectly centered wall thickness.

example_code_snippets:
' Thin-walled swept-cut with TRUE mid-plane wall thickness
Dim swApp As SldWorks.SldWorks
Dim swModel As SldWorks.ModelDoc2
Dim swFeatureMgr As SldWorks.FeatureManager
Dim swSweep As SldWorks.SweepFeatureData
Dim swFeature As SldWorks.Feature

Set swApp = Application.SldWorks
Set swModel = swApp.ActiveDoc
Set swFeatureMgr = swModel.FeatureManager

' Select the sweep profile and path
swModel.Extension.SelectByID2 "Sketch2", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, False, 1, Nothing, 0    ' Profile
swModel.Extension.SelectByID2 "Sketch3", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, True, 4, Nothing, 0     ' Path

' Create the sweep cut feature definition
Set swSweep = swFeatureMgr.CreateDefinition(swFmSweepCut)

' Configure for thin-walled with MID-PLANE geometry (ThinWallType = 2)
swSweep.ThinFeature = True
swSweep.ThinWallType = 2                  ' swThinWallMidPlane - symmetric about profile centerline
swSweep.SetWallThickness True, 0.001      ' Direction 1: 0.001m total (0.0005m on one side)
swSweep.SetWallThickness False, 0.001     ' Direction 2: 0.001m total (0.0005m on other side)
                                          ' Total wall = 0.001m centered on profile

' Configure orientation and alignment
swSweep.TwistControlType = 0              ' swTwistControlFollowPath
swSweep.PathAlignmentType = 0             ' swTangencyNone
swSweep.AlignWithEndFaces = True

' Additional options
swSweep.TangentPropagation = False
swSweep.MaintainTangency = False
swSweep.AdvancedSmoothing = False
swSweep.StartTangencyType = 0
swSweep.EndTangencyType = 0
swSweep.FeatureScope = True
swSweep.AutoSelect = True
swSweep.SetTwistAngle 0#
swSweep.MergeSmoothFaces = True
swSweep.CircularProfile = False
swSweep.Direction = 0

' Create the feature
Set swFeature = swFeatureMgr.CreateFeature(swSweep)

If Not swFeature Is Nothing Then
    Debug.Print "Created feature: " & swFeature.Name
    
    ' Verify thin-walled configuration
    Set swSweep = swFeature.GetDefinition
    swSweep.AccessSelections swModel, Nothing
    Debug.Print "Is thin-walled: " & swSweep.IsThinFeature
    Debug.Print "Thin wall type: " & swSweep.ThinWallType & " (2=MidPlane, 3=TwoDirection)"
    Debug.Print "Wall thickness D1: " & swSweep.GetWallThickness(True) * 1000 & " mm"
    Debug.Print "Wall thickness D2: " & swSweep.GetWallThickness(False) * 1000 & " mm"
    swSweep.ReleaseSelectionAccess
End If

description: Creates a swept-cut feature with thin-walled characteristics using two-direction wall thickness (swThinWallTwoDirection), where wall thickness is applied in both forward and reverse directions perpendicular to the sketch profile plane. This is configured by enabling ThinFeature, setting ThinWallType to 3, and specifying wall thicknesses for both directions using SetWallThickness(). Note: This refers to wall thickness direction, not the sweep path direction controlled by the Direction property.

description: Surface sweep features create non-solid surface geometry by sweeping a profile sketch or curve along a path sketch or edge. Created using IFeatureManager::CreateDefinition with swFmRefSurface enum value. Unlike boss and cut sweeps, surface sweeps cannot be thin-walled and do not merge with solid bodies. The ISweepFeatureData interface provides control over profile orientation, twist behavior, guide curves, and tangency options.

description: This use case documents how to create a sweep feature with guide curves. Guide curves are optional curve references (sketches, edges, or curves) that influence how the profile twists and orients as it sweeps along the path. Guide curves are pre-selected with Mark=2 before calling CreateFeature. The TwistControlType property determines how guide curves affect the sweep: swTwistControlFollowPathFirstGuideCurve (requires ≥1 guide curve) uses the first guide curve to control twist, and swTwistControlFollowFirstSecondGuideCurves (requires ≥2 guide curves) uses both first and second guide curves. Guide curves cannot be used with bidirectional sweeps.

example_code_snippets:
' Example 1: Create sweep boss with one guide curve
' Pre-select profile, path, and guide curve
status = swModelDocExt.SelectByID2("Sketch1", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, False, 1, Nothing, 0)  ' Profile
status = swModelDocExt.SelectByID2("Sketch2", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, True, 4, Nothing, 0)  ' Path
status = swModelDocExt.SelectByID2("Sketch3", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, True, 2, Nothing, 0)  ' Guide curve

Set swSweep = swFeatureMgr.CreateDefinition(swFmSweep)

' Configure to follow path with first guide curve
swSweep.TwistControlType = swTwistControlFollowPathFirstGuideCurve  ' Value = 2
swSweep.PathAlignmentType = swTangencyNone  ' Value = 0
swSweep.MergeSmoothFaces = True
swSweep.TangentPropagation = False
swSweep.AlignWithEndFaces = False
swSweep.Merge = True
swSweep.AutoSelect = True

Set swFeature = swFeatureMgr.CreateFeature(swSweep)

' Example 2: Query guide curves from existing sweep feature
Set swSweep = swFeature.GetDefinition
bRet = swSweep.AccessSelections(swModel, Nothing)

nbrGuideCurves = swSweep.GetGuideCurvesCount
Debug.Print "Number of guide curves: " & nbrGuideCurves

Dim i As Long
For i = 0 To (nbrGuideCurves - 1)
    guideCurveType = swSweep.GetGuideCurvesType(i)
    Debug.Print "  Guide curve " & i & " type (swSelectType_e): " & guideCurveType
Next i

guideCurves = swSweep.GuideCurves  ' Get array of guide curve objects
swSweep.ReleaseSelectionAccess

' Example 3: Sweep surface with two guide curves
status = swModelDocExt.SelectByID2("Sketch1", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, False, 1, Nothing, 0)
status = swModelDocExt.SelectByID2("Sketch2", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, True, 4, Nothing, 0)
status = swModelDocExt.SelectByID2("Sketch3", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, True, 2, Nothing, 0)  ' First guide
status = swModelDocExt.SelectByID2("Sketch4", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, True, 2, Nothing, 0)  ' Second guide

Set swSweep = swFeatureMgr.CreateDefinition(swFmRefSurface)

' Use both guide curves for orientation control
swSweep.TwistControlType = swTwistControlFollowFirstSecondGuideCurves  ' Value = 3
swSweep.PathAlignmentType = swTangencyNone
swSweep.TangentPropagation = False
swSweep.MaintainTangency = False
swSweep.AdvancedSmoothing = False
swSweep.MergeSmoothFaces = True

Set swFeature = swFeatureMgr.CreateFeature(swSweep)

description: A sweep feature can be configured to control tangency at the start and end of the sweep path using StartTangencyType and EndTangencyType properties. Setting these to swTangencyNormalToProfile aligns the profile normal to the path at the endpoints, but this option is only valid for 2D paths. These properties are distinct from PathAlignmentType (which controls profile orientation along the entire path) and MaintainTangency (which controls merging of tangent faces in the resulting geometry).

specialized_signature:
' Create sweep feature definition (swFmSweep, swFmSweepCut, or swFmRefSurface)
obj = feature_mgr.CreateDefinition(swFmSweep)  ' or swFmSweepCut, swFmRefSurface

' Profile and Path (set via pre-selection or by accessing with AccessSelections)
' obj.Profile -> selected sketch profile or tool body (Feature reference)
' obj.Path -> selected sweep path (Feature reference)

' Tangency control at start and end of path (2D PATHS ONLY)
' IMPORTANT: swTangencyNormalToProfile only valid for 2D paths
obj.StartTangencyType = swTangencyNone           ' 0 = no tangency control (default)
obj.StartTangencyType = swTangencyNormalToProfile ' 1 = align profile normal to path at start (2D paths only)

obj.EndTangencyType = swTangencyNone             ' 0 = no tangency control (default) 
obj.EndTangencyType = swTangencyNormalToProfile   ' 1 = align profile normal to path at end (2D paths only)

' Profile twist/orientation control (separate from endpoint tangency)
obj.TwistControlType = swTwistControlFollowPath   ' 0 = follow path orientation
obj.PathAlignmentType = swTangencyNone            ' 0 = no special alignment
' Note: PathAlignmentType controls profile orientation along entire path,
'       not endpoint tangency. See TwistControlType/PathAlignmentType documentation
'       for complex interaction table.

' Tangent face merging (affects resulting geometry, not endpoint tangency)
obj.MaintainTangency = False  ' merge tangent faces when profile has tangent segments

' Other commonly used properties
obj.TangentPropagation = False    ' propagate to tangent edges
obj.AlignWithEndFaces = False     ' align with end faces
obj.AdvancedSmoothing = False     ' advanced smoothing
obj.AutoSelect = True             ' auto-select bodies in multibody
obj.FeatureScope = True           ' use scope in multibody
obj.Merge = True                  ' merge results (boss features)
obj.MergeSmoothFaces = True       ' merge smooth faces with guide curves
obj.CircularProfile = False       ' use circular profile
obj.ThinFeature = False           ' thin-walled feature

' Ignored/deprecated properties
' - StartDirectionVector (obsolete)
' - EndDirectionVector (obsolete)

' Constraints for StartTangencyType and EndTangencyType
' - swTangencyNormalToProfile ONLY valid for 2D paths
' - NOT valid if Direction = swSweepBiDirectional
' - NOT valid if TwistControlType = swTwistControlConstantTwistAlongPath
' - Valid enum values:
'   * swTangencyNone (0) - no tangency control at endpoint
'   * swTangencyNormalToProfile (1) - align normal to path (2D only)
example_code_snippets:
```vb
' Example 1: Create sweep with tangency control at endpoints (2D PATH REQUIRED)
Dim swFeatMgr As SldWorks.FeatureManager
Dim swSweep As SldWorks.SweepFeatureData
Dim swFeature As SldWorks.Feature

Set swFeatMgr = swModel.FeatureManager

' Pre-select profile (mark 1) and path (mark 4)
' NOTE: Path must be 2D for swTangencyNormalToProfile to work
swModel.Extension.SelectByID2("Sketch1", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, False, 1, Nothing, 0)
swModel.Extension.SelectByID2("Sketch2", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, True, 4, Nothing, 0)

Set swSweep = swFeatMgr.CreateDefinition(swFmSweep)

' Set endpoint tangency (only valid for 2D paths)
swSweep.StartTangencyType = swTangencyNormalToProfile  ' 1 = normal to path at start
swSweep.EndTangencyType = swTangencyNormalToProfile    ' 1 = normal to path at end

' Set twist control
swSweep.TwistControlType = swTwistControlFollowPath    ' 0
swSweep.PathAlignmentType = swTangencyNone              ' 0

' Other properties
swSweep.TangentPropagation = False
swSweep.MaintainTangency = False  ' controls tangent face merging, not endpoint tangency
swSweep.AutoSelect = True

Set swFeature = swFeatMgr.CreateFeature(swSweep)
```

```vb
' Example 2: Query endpoint tangency from existing sweep
Dim swSweepData As SldWorks.SweepFeatureData
Set swSweepData = swFeature.GetDefinition
bRet = swSweepData.AccessSelections(swModel, Nothing)

Debug.Print "Start tangency: " & swSweepData.StartTangencyType  ' 0=None, 1=NormalToProfile
Debug.Print "End tangency: " & swSweepData.EndTangencyType      ' 0=None, 1=NormalToProfile
Debug.Print "Path type: " & swSweepData.GetPathType             ' Check if 2D path

swSweepData.ReleaseSelectionAccess
```

```vb  
' Example 3: Sweep with no endpoint tangency control (default)
Set swSweep = swFeatMgr.CreateDefinition(swFmSweepCut)

swSweep.StartTangencyType = swTangencyNone  ' 0 (default)
swSweep.EndTangencyType = swTangencyNone    ' 0 (default)
swSweep.TwistControlType = swTwistControlFollowPath

Set swFeature = swFeatMgr.CreateFeature(swSweep)
```

description: Creates a multiface draft feature with a neutral plane and no face propagation using IFeatureManager::InsertMultiFaceDraft. This method requires pre-selecting geometry with specific selection marks using SelectByID2 before calling: neutral plane/direction reference (mark=1), faces to draft (mark=2), and optional edges for edge draft (mark=4). The PropType parameter is set to 0 (swFacePropNone) to disable automatic face propagation. This is a direct insertion method that does not use the CreateDefinition/CreateFeature pattern.

description: Creates a multiface edge draft feature using IFeatureManager::InsertMultiFaceDraft, which applies a draft angle to selected edges with automatic propagation to adjacent tangent faces that share a neutral plane. When EdgeDraft=true, users select edges and SOLIDWORKS automatically identifies the adjacent faces to draft. PropType=1 is specifically valid for edge draft and neutral plane draft modes.

description: Creates a neutral plane multiface draft feature that propagates to all faces sharing edges with the neutral plane. This uses InsertMultiFaceDraft with PropType=2 (swFacePropAllLoops) to automatically include all neighboring faces adjacent to the neutral plane. The method requires pre-selection of the neutral plane face (mark 1) and initial faces to draft (mark 2) using SelectByID2.

description: Creates a multiface draft feature with propagation to all neighbor faces on inner loops (e.g., all faces of a pocket or boss). The PropType parameter value of 3 causes automatic propagation of the draft angle to neighbor faces within inner loop boundaries. The method directly creates the feature without using the CreateDefinition/CreateFeature pattern, requiring pre-selections via SelectByID2 with specific selection marks: mark 1 for direction reference (planar face, reference plane, edge, or two vertices), mark 2 for faces to draft, and mark 4 for draft edges (if EdgeDraft=true). Note: PropType accepts raw integer values (0-4) that correspond to but are distinct from the swDraftFacePropagationType_e enum used by IDraftFeatureData2::FacePropagation property.

description: Creates a multiface draft feature with propagation to neighbor faces on the outer loop using InsertMultiFaceDraft. This method creates a neutral plane draft (Type=swNeutralPlaneDraft=0) that automatically propagates to all faces neighboring the neutral plane on the outer loop (e.g., the four sides of a box when the bottom face is the neutral plane). The method requires pre-selections via SelectByID2: selection mark 1 for the direction face or neutral plane, and selection mark 2 for the initial face(s) to draft. The PropType=4 parameter controls outer loop propagation and corresponds to swFacePropOuterLoops in the swDraftFacePropagationType_e enum.

description: Inserts a multiface step draft feature directly using IFeatureManager::InsertMultiFaceDraft with IsStepDraft=true. Step drafts taper faces perpendicular to or at an angle from a neutral plane. This method requires pre-selection of the direction reference (mark=1), faces to draft (mark=2), and optionally draft edges (mark=4) using SelectByID2. After creation, the StepType property (swDraftPerpendicular=6 or swDraftTaperedStep=3) can be accessed or modified via GetDefinition/ModifyDefinition to control whether the step is perpendicular or tapered.

description: Creates a multiface draft feature that tapers faces of a body by applying a draft angle. This method supports three draft types (neutral plane, parting line, and step draft) determined by the selections and parameters. Requires pre-selections using SelectByID2: selection mark 1 for draft direction (face, plane, edge, or vertices), selection mark 2 for faces to draft (when EdgeDraft=false), or selection mark 4 for edges to draft (when EdgeDraft=true). The resulting draft type and available properties depend on the combination of selections and method parameters.

description: Creates a neutral plane draft feature using InsertMultiFaceDraft. This is a specific use case where the draft is applied relative to a selected neutral plane (selection mark 1), resulting in a draft feature with Type=swNeutralPlaneDraft (0). The neutral plane serves as the reference from which draft angles are measured, and various face propagation modes control which adjacent faces are drafted.

description: A parting line draft applies draft angles to faces using edges (parting lines) as the draft direction reference. The parting line edges are selected with mark 1 and define the draft direction, while faces to be drafted are selected with mark 2. The draft propagates from the parting line based on the specified propagation type. For multi-segment parting lines, each segment can optionally have different draft directions using the 'Other Face' option.
source_documents: [InsertMultiFaceDraft.md] - Method signature with parameters: angle (radians), flipDir (bool), edgeDraft (bool - false for face draft), propType (int 0-4 maps to swDraftFacePropagationType_e), isStepDraft (bool - false for parting line), isBodyDraft (bool). Selection marks: mark 1 = direction reference (for parting line draft, this is the parting line edges), mark 2 = faces to draft, mark 4 = draft edges (for edge draft mode only)

description: A step draft with perpendicular step type is a multiface draft feature that creates a perpendicular step between draft sections. This requires a two-phase workflow: first call InsertMultiFaceDraft with IsStepDraft=true to create the feature, then use GetDefinition/AccessSelections to modify the StepType property to swDraftPerpendicular (6), and finally call ModifyDefinition to apply the changes. The perpendicular step type creates a step at a right angle to the draft direction.

description: Creates a multiface draft feature with step draft enabled using InsertMultiFaceDraft. When IsStepDraft=true, the resulting draft is a step draft with StepType determined by SOLIDWORKS (the exact mechanism for controlling whether it becomes swDraftTaperedStep=3 vs swDraftPerpendicular=6 is not documented in the current API). Properties can be queried via IDraftFeatureData2 after creation and potentially modified using the AccessSelections/ModifyDefinition pattern.

description: Modifying an existing draft feature by retrieving its definition using IFeature::GetDefinition, updating properties on the IDraftFeatureData2 object, and applying changes via IFeature::ModifyDefinition. This pattern enables programmatic adjustment of draft angles, face propagation, step types, and other draft parameters. For properties involving geometric selections (faces, edges, planes), AccessSelections must be called first to put the model into rollback state, then either ModifyDefinition (to apply changes) or ReleaseSelectionAccess (to cancel) must be called to restore the model state.

description: A single-ended extruded boss or base feature with a blind (fixed depth) end condition. This creates a solid boss by extruding a selected sketch profile in one direction to a specified depth. The extrusion starts from the sketch plane (or optionally from an offset/surface) and extends until the blind depth is reached. This is the most fundamental extrusion type, supporting optional draft angles, multibody merging, and body scoping. For 3D sketches, additional selection requirements apply.

Includes critical remarks about direction defaults and 3D sketch selection requirements.

description: This feature case creates a single-ended extruded boss or base feature with a through-all end condition, where the boss extrusion extends from the sketch plane along the default direction (perpendicular to sketch plane, along sketch normal) and continues until it completely passes through all existing solid material. This is useful for creating projections or bosses that extend through the entire depth of existing geometry without specifying an exact depth value. The feature adds material (boss operation) rather than removing it.

description: Single-ended boss/base extrusion with up-to-next end condition creates a boss or base feature that extrudes from a sketch in one direction until it reaches the next intersecting face or surface in the extrusion path. Unlike swEndCondThroughNext which penetrates through the first face, swEndCondUpToNext stops at it. This requires selecting the sketch (Mark=0) before calling FeatureExtrusion3 with T1=swEndCondUpToNext (value 11). Note: The depth parameter D1 is ignored as the termination distance is determined by geometry. Macro recording may incorrectly record swEndCondThroughNext (2) instead of swEndCondUpToNext (11).

description: Creates a single-ended boss or base extrusion feature that terminates at a specified offset distance from a reference surface or plane. The extrusion starts from a sketch plane, reference surface, or vertex (controlled by T0 parameter) and extends in one direction until reaching an offset distance from the end condition reference surface. Critical selections include: sketch (mark 0), start condition reference entity (mark 32 when T0 equals swStartSurface or swStartVertex), and end condition reference surface/plane (mark 1). The offset direction (toward or away from sketch) is controlled by OffsetReverse1, and TranslateSurface1 determines whether the end uses surface translation or true offset calculation.

description: Creates a double-ended boss or base extrusion feature where each end can have independent termination conditions (T1/T2), depths (D1/D2), draft angles (Dang1/Dang2), and offset behaviors. When using IExtrudeFeatureData2, set BothDirections=true and configure each direction independently using Forward=true for Direction 1 and Forward=false for Direction 2. Note that Direction 2 always extrudes opposite to Direction 1.

description: Boss/base extrusion with draft angle applied allows creating extruded features with single-ended or double-ended configurations, configurable end conditions, and draft angles applied during the extrusion process. For 2D sketches, the default extrusion direction is along the sketch normal for boss operations and opposite for cuts. For 3D sketches, an extrusion direction edge must be selected. Draft angles can be applied in forward and/or reverse directions, with control over inward vs outward drafting. This feature integrates with the start and end condition system, supporting blind, through-all, offset-from-surface, and other termination types.

specialized_signature:
// SELECTION MARKS: For 3D sketches use mark 0 for sketch, 16 for direction edge, 32 for start reference, 1 for end reference, 8 for bodies when UseAutoSelect=false

IFeature feature = featureManager.FeatureExtrusion3(
    Sd: true,  // Single-ended
    Flip: false,
    Dir: true,  // For 2D: flips sketch normal. For 3D: direction from selected edge (mark 16)
    T1: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    T2: 0,  // Ignored if single-ended
    D1: 0.01,  // Depth in meters
    D2: 0.0,
    Dchk1: true,  // MUST be true to enable Dang1/Ddir1
    Dchk2: false,
    Ddir1: false,  // false=outward, true=inward; valid only if Dchk1=true
    Ddir2: false,
    Dang1: 0.0174532925199433,  // Radians; valid only if Dchk1=true
    Dang2: 0.0,
    OffsetReverse1: false,  // Valid only if T1=swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
    OffsetReverse2: false,
    TranslateSurface1: false,  // Valid only if T1=swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
    TranslateSurface2: false,
    Merge: true,
    UseFeatScope: false,  // true=affects only selected bodies
    UseAutoSelect: true,  // false requires body selection with mark 8
    T0: (int)swStartConditions_e.swStartSketchPlane,
    StartOffset: 0.0,  // Valid only if T0=swStartOffset
    FlipStartOffset: false
);

// Alternative: IExtrudeFeatureData2
extrudeData.SetDraftWhileExtruding(true, true);  // Enable draft forward
extrudeData.SetDraftOutward(true, false);  // false=inward
extrudeData.SetDraftAngle(true, 0.0174532925199433);

description: Creates an extruded boss or base feature starting from an offset distance relative to the sketch plane using the swStartOffset start condition (T0=3). The StartOffset parameter specifies the distance in meters, and FlipStartOffset controls the offset direction relative to the sketch normal (false=along normal, true=opposite normal). This is equivalent to setting IExtrudeFeatureData2::FromType to swExtrudeFrom_Offset. Supports single-ended or double-ended extrusions with configurable end conditions and draft angles. Note: swStartSurface and swStartVertex require reference entity selection with mark 32.

description: This feature case represents the creation of an extruded thin boss or base feature with configurable wall thickness. It supports single-ended or double-ended extrusions, four thin wall types (one direction, one direction reverse, mid-plane, or two direction), optional end caps for base features, and draft angles. The AddBends parameter enables automatic bend creation for open profile sheet metal base features. For automatic corner filleting of thin features, use IExtrudeFeatureData2::SetAutoFillet after feature creation.

description: Creates a double-ended thin boss or base extrusion where material extends in both directions from the sketch plane with mid-plane thickness distribution. The mid-plane thickness type (RevThinDir=2) causes Thk1 to be distributed symmetrically as Thk1/2 on each side of the sketch plane, while Thk2 is ignored. Supports independent depths, end conditions, and draft angles for each direction.

var thinExtrude = featureManager.FeatureExtrusionThin2(
    Sd: false,
    Flip: false,
    Dir: false,
    T1: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    T2: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    D1: 0.00254,
    D2: 0.00254,
    Dchk1: false,
    Dchk2: false,
    Ddir1: false,
    Ddir2: false,
    Dang1: 0.0,
    Dang2: 0.0,
    OffsetReverse1: false,
    OffsetReverse2: false,
    TranslateSurface1: false,
    TranslateSurface2: false,
    Merge: true,
    Thk1: 0.001,
    Thk2: 0.0,
    EndThk: 0.0,
    RevThinDir: 2,
    CapEnds: 0,
    AddBends: false,
    BendRad: 0.0,
    UseFeatScope: true,
    UseAutoSelect: true,
    T0: (int)swStartConditions_e.swStartSketchPlane,
    StartOffset: 0.0,
    FlipStartOffset: false
);

IExtrudeFeatureData2 data = (IExtrudeFeatureData2)thinExtrude.GetDefinition();
data.AccessSelections(modelDoc, null);
double fwdThickness = data.GetWallThickness(true);
double revThickness = data.GetWallThickness(false);
data.ReleaseSelectionAccess();

description: Creates a thin-walled extruded boss or base feature with configurable thickness, end conditions, draft angles, and optional end caps using IFeatureManager::FeatureExtrusionThin2. Supports single-ended or double-ended extrusions with multiple thickness configurations (one direction, reverse direction, mid-plane, or two-direction). Features optional automatic corner fillets for open profile base features and adjustable end cap thickness for capped thin extrusions.

description: This feature case creates a single-ended cut extrusion with blind end condition, which is the simplest form of cut extrusion. The cut extends from the sketch plane into the material for a specified depth (D1 parameter). When Sd=true (single-ended), all second-end parameters (T2, D2, Dchk2, Ddir2, Dang2, OffsetReverse2, TranslateSurface2) are ignored. The blind end condition (swEndCondBlind=0) directly uses the D1 depth value without requiring any reference entities, making it straightforward to implement. The sketch must be selected before calling FeatureCut4.

description: A single-ended cut extrusion feature that removes material completely through the entire part using the through-all end condition. This type of cut is created using FeatureCut4 with single-ended mode enabled and the first end condition set to swEndCondThroughAll (value 1). When using through-all, the D1 depth parameter is ignored since the cut extends through all intersecting geometry. This is ideal for creating holes, slots, and cutouts that must extend completely through the solid body regardless of part thickness.

description: Creates a double-ended cut extrude feature via direct IFeatureManager::FeatureCut4 method call, supporting independent end conditions, depths, and draft angles for each direction. This method is called directly with 27 parameters including separate termination types (T1/T2), depths (D1/D2), and draft settings (Dchk1/Dchk2, Ddir1/Ddir2, Dang1/Dang2 in radians) for each direction, plus specialized options for sheet metal normal cuts, offset surface translation, feature scope for multibody parts, and assembly feature propagation.

specialized_signature:
// Create double-ended cut extrude via direct FeatureCut4 call
IFeatureManager featureMgr = modelDoc.FeatureManager;

// Select sketch before calling FeatureCut4
modelDoc.Extension.SelectByID2("Sketch1", "SKETCH", 0, 0, 0, false, 0, null, 0);

// Call FeatureCut4 with 27 parameters
IFeature cutFeature = featureMgr.FeatureCut4(
    false,  // Sd: false=double-ended, true=single-ended
    false,  // Flip: true removes material outside profile
    false,  // Dir: true reverses default direction
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,  // T1: direction 1 end condition
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondThroughAll,  // T2: direction 2 end condition
    0.010,  // D1: direction 1 depth in meters
    0.015,  // D2: direction 2 depth in meters
    true,   // Dchk1: enable draft direction 1
    false,  // Ddir1: false=outward, true=inward
    0.0873, // Dang1: draft angle in RADIANS (5 degrees * PI/180)
    true,   // Dchk2: enable draft direction 2
    true,   // Ddir2: false=outward, true=inward
    0.0349, // Dang2: draft angle in RADIANS (2 degrees * PI/180)
    false,  // OffsetReverse1: for swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
    false,  // OffsetReverse2: for swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
    false,  // TranslateSurface1: for swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
    false,  // TranslateSurface2: for swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
    false,  // NormalCut: true for sheet metal only
    false,  // UseFeatScope: true to affect only selected bodies
    true,   // UseAutoSelect: false requires manual selection with mark 8
    false,  // AssemblyFeatureScope: true for selected components
    true,   // AutoSelectComponents: true auto-selects components
    false,  // PropagateFeatureToParts: true propagates to parts
    (int)swStartConditions_e.swStartSketchPlane,  // T0: start condition
    0.0,    // StartOffset: distance when T0=swStartOffset
    false,  // FlipStartOffset: flip when T0=swStartOffset
    false   // OptimizeGeometry: only valid when NormalCut=true
);

description: Creates a cut extrusion feature with the flip side to cut option enabled. For closed sketch profiles, this determines whether material inside or outside the profile is removed. For open sketch profiles, the first edge returned by ISketch::GetContourEdges defines the cut boundary. This feature supports single-ended or double-ended cuts, configurable end conditions, draft angles, normal cuts for sheet metal parts, and assembly feature scoping.

example_code_snippets:
```csharp
// Example 1: Cut with closed profile and flip side to cut
// Removes material OUTSIDE the closed sketch profile
using SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks;
using SolidWorks.Interop.swconst;

// ... create closed rectangle sketch ...

Feature cutFeature = featureManager.FeatureCut4(
    sd: true,           // Single-ended
    flip: true,         // Remove material OUTSIDE profile (closed sketch)
    dir: false,         // Default direction (opposite sketch normal)
    t1: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    t2: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    d1: 0.01,          // 10mm depth
    d2: 0.0,
    dchk1: false, dchk2: false, ddir1: false, ddir2: false,
    dang1: 0.0, dang2: 0.0,
    offsetReverse1: false, offsetReverse2: false,
    translateSurface1: false, translateSurface2: false,
    normalCut: false,
    useFeatScope: false, useAutoSelect: false,
    assemblyFeatureScope: false, autoSelectComponents: false,
    propagateFeatureToParts: false,
    t0: (int)swStartConditions_e.swStartSketchPlane,
    startOffset: 0.0, flipStartOffset: false,
    optimizeGeometry: false
);

// Example 2: Cut with OPEN profile and flip side to cut
// The first edge from GetContourEdges defines the cut boundary

// ... create open sketch (e.g., line or arc) ...

// Get sketch to understand edge order for open profiles
Sketch sketch = (Sketch)model.GetActiveSketch2();
object[] edges = (object[])sketch.GetContourEdges(null);
Edge firstEdge = (Edge)edges[0];  // This edge defines cut when flip=true

Feature openCutFeature = featureManager.FeatureCut4(
    sd: true,
    flip: true,         // First edge from GetContourEdges is the cut boundary
    dir: false,
    t1: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    t2: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    d1: 0.005,
    d2: 0.0,
    dchk1: false, dchk2: false, ddir1: false, ddir2: false,
    dang1: 0.0, dang2: 0.0,
    offsetReverse1: false, offsetReverse2: false,
    translateSurface1: false, translateSurface2: false,
    normalCut: false,
    useFeatScope: false, useAutoSelect: false,
    assemblyFeatureScope: false, autoSelectComponents: false,
    propagateFeatureToParts: false,
    t0: (int)swStartConditions_e.swStartSketchPlane,
    startOffset: 0.0, flipStartOffset: false,
    optimizeGeometry: false
);

// Example 3: Verify FlipSideToCut property
ExtrudeFeatureData2 data = (ExtrudeFeatureData2)cutFeature.GetDefinition();
bool isFlipped = data.FlipSideToCut;  // Returns true
// Note: FlipSideToCut is only relevant for cut features, not boss extrudes

// Example 4: Double-ended cut with flip
Feature doubleEndedCut = featureManager.FeatureCut4(
    sd: false,          // Double-ended (BothDirections=true)
    flip: true,         // Flip side to cut enabled
    dir: false,
    t1: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    t2: (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,
    d1: 0.005,         // 5mm forward
    d2: 0.005,         // 5mm reverse
    dchk1: false, dchk2: false, ddir1: false, ddir2: false,
    dang1: 0.0, dang2: 0.0,
    offsetReverse1: false, offsetReverse2: false,
    translateSurface1: false, translateSurface2: false,
    normalCut: false,
    useFeatScope: false, useAutoSelect: false,
    assemblyFeatureScope: false, autoSelectComponents: false,
    propagateFeatureToParts: false,
    t0: (int)swStartConditions_e.swStartSketchPlane,
    startOffset: 0.0, flipStartOffset: false,
    optimizeGeometry: false
);
```

description: This feature case creates a cut extrusion in sheet metal parts with the ability to create a cut normal (perpendicular) to the sheet metal thickness, regardless of sketch orientation. The cut direction defaults to opposite the sketch normal for cut operations. The feature supports single-ended or double-ended cuts with configurable end conditions, draft angles, and optional geometry optimization specifically for sheet metal manufacturing (punches, laser cuts, etc.). The OptimizeGeometry option is only valid when NormalCut is enabled and helps optimize the resulting geometry for sheet metal fabrication.

description: Creates a cut extrusion feature with draft angle support for single-ended or double-ended cuts. Draft angles are controlled by three parameter groups per direction: Dchk (enable/disable), Ddir (inward/outward), and Dang (angle in radians). The Dchk parameters act as gates - when Dchk1=false, both Ddir1 and Dang1 are ignored. Supports configurable end conditions, flip side to cut, normal cuts for sheet metal, and assembly feature options.

description: Creates a cut extrude feature in an assembly or multibody part with feature scope to affect only selected bodies or components. Supports single/double-ended cuts, draft angles, normal cuts for sheet metal, offset start conditions, and assembly feature propagation. When UseFeatScope=true and UseAutoSelect=false, only bodies pre-selected with selection mark 8 are affected. For assemblies, AssemblyFeatureScope enables component-level targeting with similar behavior.

specialized_signature:
// IMPORTANT: Pre-select entities with marks BEFORE calling FeatureCut4:
//   - Bodies to affect (mark 8): when UseFeatScope=true, UseAutoSelect=false
//   - 3D sketch extrusion: sketch (mark 0), direction edge (mark 16)
//   - Start reference (mark 32): when T0=swStartSurface/swStartVertex
//   - End reference (mark 1): when T1/T2=swEndCondUpToSelection/swEndCondOffsetFromSurface/swEndCondUpToBody

Feature feature = featureManager.FeatureCut4(
    bool Sd,              // true=single-ended, false=double-ended
    bool Flip,            // true=remove outside profile, false=remove inside
    bool Dir,             // true=reverse direction (same as sketch normal), false=default (opposite sketch normal)
    int T1,               // First end: swEndConditions_e (0-blind, 1-throughAll, 2-throughNext, 10-upToSelection, etc.)
    int T2,               // Second end: swEndConditions_e (ignored if Sd=true)
    double D1,            // Depth/offset for first end (meters)
    double D2,            // Depth/offset for second end (meters)
    bool Dchk1,           // Enable draft on first end
    bool Dchk2,           // Enable draft on second end
    bool Ddir1,           // true=inward draft, false=outward (if Dchk1=true)
    bool Ddir2,           // true=inward draft, false=outward (if Dchk2=true)
    double Dang1,         // Draft angle first end (radians, if Dchk1=true)
    double Dang2,         // Draft angle second end (radians, if Dchk2=true)
    bool OffsetReverse1,  // Offset away from sketch (if T1=swEndCondOffsetFromSurface)
    bool OffsetReverse2,  // Offset away from sketch (if T2=swEndCondOffsetFromSurface)
    bool TranslateSurface1, // true=translate, false=true offset (if T1=swEndCondOffsetFromSurface)
    bool TranslateSurface2, // true=translate, false=true offset (if T2=swEndCondOffsetFromSurface)
    bool NormalCut,       // true=cut normal to sheet metal thickness (sheet metal only)
    bool UseFeatScope,    // true=affect only selected/intersecting bodies, false=all bodies
    bool UseAutoSelect,   // true=auto-select intersecting bodies, false=use pre-selected mark 8 (requires UseFeatScope=true)
    bool AssemblyFeatureScope,    // true=affect only selected components, false=all components
    bool AutoSelectComponents,    // true=auto-select components, false=use selected (requires AssemblyFeatureScope=true)
    bool PropagateFeatureToParts, // true=propagate to component files, false=assembly only
    int T0,               // Start: swStartConditions_e (0-sketchPlane, 1-surface, 2-vertex, 3-offset)
    double StartOffset,   // Offset distance (meters, if T0=swStartOffset)
    bool FlipStartOffset, // Flip offset direction (if T0=swStartOffset)
    bool OptimizeGeometry // Optimize cut geometry (if NormalCut=true)
);

// Returns: Feature or null if failed

// Feature Scope: UseFeatScope=true, UseAutoSelect=false requires bodies pre-selected with mark 8
// Assembly Scope: AssemblyFeatureScope=true, AutoSelectComponents=false requires components pre-selected

description: A single-ended thin cut extrude feature that removes material from a solid body by cutting a thin groove or slot with a specified wall thickness. The cut extends in one direction from the sketch plane, removing material in a thin profile rather than the full sketch profile. Unlike thin boss features that add material, thin cuts remove material in the direction and thickness specified by the wall thickness parameters.

specialized_signature:
// For single-ended thin cut extrusion with wall thickness
// Creates a groove/slot by removing material in a thin profile
Feature feature = featureManager.FeatureCutThin2(
    true,                                          // Sd: true for single-ended, false for double-ended
    false,                                         // Flip: true to remove material outside profile, false to remove inside
    false,                                         // Dir: true to reverse default direction (default is opposite sketch normal for cuts)
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,        // T1: End condition for first end
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondBlind,        // T2: IGNORED for single-ended cuts
    0.01,                                          // D1: Depth of extrusion for first end in meters
    0.01,                                          // D2: IGNORED for single-ended
    false,                                         // Dchk1: true to enable draft angle in first direction
    false,                                         // Dchk2: IGNORED for single-ended
    false,                                         // Ddir1: true for inward draft, false for outward
    false,                                         // Ddir2: IGNORED for single-ended
    0.01745329251994,                              // Dang1: Draft angle in radians
    0.01745329251994,                              // Dang2: IGNORED for single-ended
    false,                                         // OffsetReverse1: If T1 is swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
    false,                                         // OffsetReverse2: IGNORED for single-ended
    false,                                         // TranslateSurface1: If T1 is swEndCondOffsetFromSurface
    false,                                         // TranslateSurface2: IGNORED for single-ended
    0.01,                                          // Thk1: Wall thickness in meters (width of groove)
    0.01,                                          // Thk2: Only used when RevThinDir=swThinWallTwoDirection(3)
    0,                                             // EndThk: NOT APPLICABLE to cut features
    (int)swThinWallType_e.swThinWallOneDirection, // RevThinDir: swThinWallOneDirection(0), swThinWallOppDirection(1), swThinWallMidPlane(2), swThinWallTwoDirection(3)
    0,                                             // CapEnds: NOT APPLICABLE to cut features
    false,                                         // AddBends: NOT APPLICABLE to cut features
    0.005,                                         // BendRad: IGNORED - not applicable to cut features
    true,                                          // UseFeatScope: true if feature only affects selected bodies
    true,                                          // UseAutoSelect: true to automatically select all bodies
    (int)swStartConditions_e.swStartSketchPlane,  // T0: Start condition
    0,                                             // StartOffset: Offset value if T0 is swStartOffset
    false                                          // FlipStartOffset: Flip direction if T0 is swStartOffset
);

// Key properties:
// - Thk1: Defines width of material removal for the groove/slot
// - RevThinDir: Controls thin wall direction using swThinWallType_e enum
// - CapEnds/EndThk/AddBends/BendRad: Not applicable to cut features
// - Accessing after creation: feature.GetDefinition().IsThinFeature() returns true

description: Creates a double-ended thin cut extrude feature that removes material symmetrically using mid-plane thickness distribution. The feature uses a sketch profile to define the cut geometry, with wall thickness applied equally on both sides of the profile (Thk1/2 per side). Double-ended operation allows asymmetric depth cuts in opposite directions from the sketch plane, with support for blind end conditions, draft angles, and customizable start conditions.

description: Creates a thin cut extrude feature that removes material with configurable wall thickness, draft angles, and side-to-cut control. The Flip parameter controls which side of the sketch profile material is removed from (true removes outside the profile, false removes inside), while the thin wall direction (RevThinDir) controls where the wall thickness is applied. For open profiles, the first edge from ISketch::GetContourEdges determines the cut side. Draft angles are applied relative to the extrusion direction. The default extrusion direction for cut operations is opposite the sketch normal, which can be reversed using the Dir parameter.
