specialized_signature:
// Method signature for FeatureCutThin2 on IFeatureManager:
Feature FeatureCutThin2(
    bool Sd,              // true: single-ended, false: double-ended
    bool Flip,            // true removes material OUTSIDE profile (for open: first edge from GetContourEdges)
    bool Dir,             // true reverses default direction (default for cuts: opposite sketch normal)
    int T1,               // End condition first end (swEndConditions_e: 0=Blind, 1=ThroughAll, etc.)
    int T2,               // End condition second end (only when Sd=false)
    double D1,            // Depth first end in meters (or offset if T1=swEndCondOffsetFromSurface)
    double D2,            // Depth second end in meters (only when Sd=false)
    bool Dchk1,           // true enables draft angle first direction
    bool Dchk2,           // true enables draft angle second direction
    bool Ddir1,           // Draft direction first: true=inward, false=outward (relative to extrusion)
    bool Ddir2,           // Draft direction second: true=inward, false=outward
    double Dang1,         // First draft angle radians (only when Dchk1=true)
    double Dang2,         // Second draft angle radians (only when Dchk2=true)
    bool OffsetReverse1,  // Reverse offset first (only when T1=swEndCondOffsetFromSurface)
    bool OffsetReverse2,  // Reverse offset second (only when T2=swEndCondOffsetFromSurface)
    bool TranslateSurface1, // Translation vs offset first (only when T1=swEndCondOffsetFromSurface)
    bool TranslateSurface2, // Translation vs offset second (only when T2=swEndCondOffsetFromSurface)
    double Thk1,          // Wall thickness 1 meters (mid-plane uses Thk1/2 per side)
    double Thk2,          // Wall thickness 2 meters (only when RevThinDir=3)
    double EndThk,        // End cap thickness meters (only when CapEnds=1)
    int RevThinDir,       // Thin type: 0=OneDir, 1=OneDirReverse, 2=MidPlane, 3=TwoDir
    int CapEnds,          // 0=no cap, 1=cap (BASE features only)
    bool AddBends,        // Auto bends (open profile base features only)
    double BendRad,       // Fillet radius meters if AddBends=true
    bool UseFeatScope,    // true: affects selected bodies only (multibody)
    bool UseAutoSelect,   // true: auto-select all bodies (when UseFeatScope=false)
    int T0,               // Start condition: 0=SketchPlane, 1=Surface, 2=Vertex, 3=Offset
    double StartOffset,   // Offset distance meters (only when T0=3)
    bool FlipStartOffset  // Flip start offset direction (only when T0=3)
);

// IExtrudeFeatureData2 properties:
obj.FlipSideToCut = true;  // true=remove outside, false=remove inside
obj.ThinWallType = 0;      // 0=OneDir, 1=OneDirRev, 2=MidPlane, 3=TwoDir
obj.SetWallThickness(true, 0.002);
obj.SetDraftWhileExtruding(true, true);
obj.SetDraftAngle(true, 0.174533);  // radians
obj.SetDraftOutward(true, false);   // false=inward
obj.CapEnds = true;  // BASE features only
obj.CapThickness = 0.001;
obj.SetEndCondition(true, 0);  // swEndCondBlind
obj.SetDepth(true, 0.01);
example_code_snippets:
// Example 1: Single-ended thin cut with flip and draft
Feature f = swFeatMgr.FeatureCutThin2(
    true,   // Single-ended
    true,   // Flip (remove outside profile)
    false,  // Normal direction
    0, 0,   // swEndCondBlind for both
    0.01, 0.01,  // Depths
    true, false, // Draft first only
    false, false, // Outward draft
    0.174533, 0,  // 10deg first, 0 second
    false, false, false, false,  // Offset/translate
    0.002, 0, 0,  // Thk1=2mm, Thk2/EndThk unused
    0,      // RevThinDir: One Direction
    0,      // No end caps
    false, 0,  // No bends
    false, true,  // No scope, auto-select
    0, 0, false   // Start from sketch plane
);

// Example 2: Double-ended mid-plane with drafts
Feature f2 = swFeatMgr.FeatureCutThin2(
    false,  // Double-ended
    true, false,  // Flip, normal dir
    0, 0, 0.005, 0.005,  // Blind both, 5mm each
    true, true,   // Draft both
    true, true,   // Inward both
    0.0873, 0.0873,  // 5deg both
    false, false, false, false,
    0.001, 0.001, 0.001,  // Thk1, Thk2, EndThk
    2,  // Mid-plane
    1,  // Cap ends
    false, 0, false, true,
    0, 0, false
);

description: Modification of existing extrude feature properties allows engineers to access and alter the geometry and parameters of previously created extrude (boss/cut) features via IFeature::GetDefinition(). This use case handles retrieving the IExtrudeFeatureData2 object from an existing feature, optionally accessing its selections (which puts the model into a rollback state), modifying properties such as depth, end conditions, direction, draft, thin feature parameters, and multibody scope, then applying changes via IFeature::ModifyDefinition() or restoring the rollback state via ReleaseSelectionAccess() if no modifications were made.

description: Create countersunk holes or slots using HoleWizard5 by specifying countersink-specific parameters including countersink diameter, angle, head clearance, and head clearance type. This feature creates a conical recessed surface for flat-head fasteners. The head clearance type determines how clearance values are applied (increased countersink vs added to counterbore).
source_documents: [SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.IFeatureManager~HoleWizard5.md] - Comprehensive HoleWizard5 method documentation with parameter specifications for countersink holes and slots; specifies Value1-Value12 parameter sequence and meanings

[SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.IFeatureManager~HoleWizard2.md] - Obsolete but clarifies countersink parameters without conditional drill angle restriction; confirms Value5=Drill Angle and Value9=Head Clearance Type
[SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.IFeatureManager~HoleWizard4.md] - Obsolete but consistent with HoleWizard2 on parameter meanings; confirms countersink parameter ordering
[Create_Countersink_Slot_Example_CSharp.md] - Official example demonstrating countersink slot creation with swEndCondThroughAll and drill angle specified (contradicts conditional restriction in docs)
[Table_of_Wizard_Hole_Types_and_Valid_Properties.md] - Valid IWizardHoleFeatureData2 properties for various countersink types
[swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.md] - General hole type enum values: swWzdCounterSink=1, swWzdCounterSinkSlot=7
[swWzdHoleCounterSinkHeadClearanceTypes_e.md] - Head clearance type enums: swHeadClearanceIncreasedCsink=0, swHeadClearanceAddToCbore=1
example_code_snippets:
// Example 1: Create countersink hole with increased countersink head clearance type
// Uses swHeadClearanceIncreasedCsink which increases the countersink dimensions
var feature = featureManager.HoleWizard5(
    (int)swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdCounterSink,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandards_e.swStandardAnsiMetric,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandardFastenerTypes_e.swStandardAnsiMetricFlatHead82,
    "M2",
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondThroughAll,
    0.0102,                  // 10.2mm hole diameter
    0.010312189893273,       // hole depth
    0,                       // not a slot
    0.0044,                  // 4.4mm near countersink diameter
    1.57079632679489,        // ~90° countersink angle
    0.000152189893272978,    // head clearance
    0,                       // normal screw fit
    2.05948851735331,        // ~118° drill angle (can specify even with Through All)
    -1, -1,                  // far csink diameter/angle (unused)
    -1,                      // offset (unused)
    (int)swWzdHoleCounterSinkHeadClearanceTypes_e.swHeadClearanceIncreasedCsink,  // head clearance type
    -1, -1, -1,              // unused values
    "",                      // no thread class
    false, true, true, true, true, false
);

// Example 2: Create countersink slot with specified length
var slotFeature = featureManager.HoleWizard5(
    (int)swWzdGeneralHoleTypes_e.swWzdCounterSinkSlot,  // slot type
    (int)swWzdHoleStandards_e.swStandardAnsiMetric,
    (int)swWzdHoleStandardFastenerTypes_e.swStandardAnsiMetricFlatHead82,
    "M2",
    (int)swEndConditions_e.swEndCondThroughAll,
    0.0102,                  // hole diameter
    0.010312189893273,       // hole depth
    1,                       // 1 meter slot length
    0.0044,                  // near countersink diameter
    1.57079632679489,        // countersink angle
    0.000152189893272978,    // head clearance
    0,                       // normal fit
    2.05948851735331,        // drill angle
    -1, -1, -1,              // far csink, offset (unused)
    0,                       // head clearance type: swHeadClearanceIncreasedCsink
    -1, -1, -1,
    "",
    false, true, true, true, true, false
);

// Example 3: Access countersink properties via IWizardHoleFeatureData2
WizardHoleFeatureData2 holeData = (WizardHoleFeatureData2)feature.GetDefinition();
var csinkDiameter = holeData.CounterSinkDiameter;  // Get countersink diameter
var csinkAngle = holeData.CounterSinkAngle;        // Get countersink angle (radians)
var headClearance = holeData.HeadClearance;        // Get head clearance
var drillAngle = holeData.DrillAngle;              // Get drill angle (radians)
var slotLength = holeData.Length;                  // Get slot length (0 for holes)

description: Creates a lofted cut feature (cavity/subtractive loft) by smoothly blending through multiple cross-sectional profiles to remove material from a solid body. Guide curves (optional) control the loft path, and a centerline (optional) controls intermediate section generation. Important: Guide curves are strongly recommended when selecting profiles from the FeatureManager. Single-profile lofts require closed guide curves. Closed lofts with fewer than three profiles also require closed guide curves. Cut lofts that create multiple bodies do not allow selective body retention.
source_documents: [SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks~SolidWorks.Interop.sldworks.IFeatureManager~InsertCutBlend.md] - InsertCutBlend method with parameters Closed, KeepTangency, ForceNonRational, TessToleranceFactor (for centerline lofts), StartMatchingType, EndMatchingType, IsThinBody, Thickness1, Thickness2, ThinType, UseFeatScope, UseAutoSelect. Selection marks: 1=profiles (required, ordered), 2=guide curves (optional), 4=centerline (optional), 8=start tangency vector, 16=start tangency faces (unavailable), 32=end tangency vector, 64=end tangency faces (unavailable). Important constraints on guide curve usage and multibody limitations.
